✅ Meningkatkan koordinasi, keseimbangan, dan kontrol gerak
✅ Mengurangi risiko cedera akibat spesialisasi dini
✅ Mempersiapkan atlet untuk performa jangka panjang

Anak-anak yang memiliki keterampilan gerak dasar yang baik, akan lebih siap menghadapi tantangan dalam latihan spesifik dan mencapai performa optimal di tingkat tinggi.

💪 TUJUAN SESEORANG RUTIN BEROLAHRAGA 💪

Ada beberapa tujuan seseorang berolaharaga, antara lain: 

1. KESEHATAN (REAKTIF / PRO-AKTIF)

- Reaktif:
Olahraga dilakukan karena terpaksa atau sebagai reaksi terhadap kondisi kesehatan yang sudah terganggu, misalnya setelah terdiagnosis kolesterol tinggi, hipertensi, diabetes, dan lain-lain. Ini berarti seseorang mulai aktif bergerak setelah ada keluhan.

- Pro-aktif:
Olahraga dilakukan secara sadar dan terencana sebagai upaya pencegahan penyakit dan pemeliharaan kondisi tubuh agar tetap sehat, meskipun belum memiliki keluhan kesehatan apa pun.

2. KEBUGARAN (PRO-AKTIF)
Tujuan olahraga adalah untuk meningkatkan kapasitas kebugaran jasmani:

- Daya tahan kardiovaskular 
- Kekuatan
- Fleksibilitas
- Komposisi tubuh

Ditujukan untuk meningkatkan kualitas hidup, produktivitas, dan fungsi fisik harian, contohnya:

- Tidak mudah lelah bekerja
- Mampu mengangkat barang berat

3. KOMPETISI (PRO-AKTIF)
Olahraga dilakukan dengan tujuan berprestasi di bidang kompetisi, baik di level rekreasional, lokal-nasional, dan level elit - internasional. 

Ciri khas:

- Latihan lebih terstruktur dan intensif
- Disusun berdasarkan prinsip periodisasi dan performa tinggi (misalnya split training, recovery strategy, peak performance)

Infografis ini juga menekankan bahwa tujuan seseorang berolahraga bisa berubah seiring waktu.

Contohnya:
Awalnya hanya untuk alasan kesehatan, namun karena konsistensi dan peningkatan kemampuan, akhirnya tujuan bisa bergeser ke kebugaran atau bahkan kompetisi.

“Berolahragalah dengan Ilmu!”

Semakin tinggi level kompetisi yang diikuti, maka semakin banyak komponen yang perlu disiapkan. Bukan hanya dari faktor langsung, melainkan juga dari faktor pendukungnya. Untuk itulah pelatih harus terus belajar dan berkolaborasi dengan banyak pihak agar peluang meraih prestasi semakin besar. 

Jika semua belum bisa dilakukan, setidaknya ada bagian-bagian yang bisa dijalankan terlebih dahulu. 

Satu langkah ke depan mendekati tujuan akan lebih baik dibandingkan hanya tetap berdiri di tempat. 

“Berolahragalah dengan Ilmu!”

#health #fitness #workout #fatloss #lifestyle

Caffeine 
Blocks adenosine receptors, spikes epinephrine/norepinephrine, and promotes fat oxidation for fuel—best in a calorie deficit (200-400mg pre-workout; e.g., coffee or pills).
TMG (Trimethylglycine) 
Donates methyl groups to recycle homocysteine, supporting methylation cycles and glucose metabolism (1-3g daily; pairs well with B-vitamins).
Fish Oil (DHA/EPA) 
DHA integrates into neuronal membranes for faster signaling; EPA curbs inflammation—aim for 1-2g combined EPA/DHA daily from high-quality sources.
Curcumin + Black Pepper (Piperine) 
Inhibits NF-κB pathway to slash systemic inflammation; piperine boosts absorption by 2000% (500-1000mg curcumin with 5-20mg piperine).
Creatine Monohydrate 
Rapidly regenerates ATP via phosphocreatine, sustaining high-intensity efforts and sparing muscle protein (3-5g daily; loading optional).
Psyllium Husk 
Swells into a gel that binds bile acids, toxins, and some particulates (including microplastics) for fecal excretion—use 5-10g with tons of water.
Magnesium Glycinate 
Boosts GABA activity for calm, buffers cortisol, and supports melatonin synthesis for deeper sleep (300-400mg elemental Mg nightly).
Glutamine 
Nourishes enterocytes to repair gut lining and tighten junctions, reducing leaky gut permeability (5-10g daily, especially post-training).

🥙 High-Protein Ramadan Diet Plan

🌅 Suhoor (Pre-Dawn Meal) – Slow & Sustained Energy

Focus on slow-digesting protein + complex carbs + healthy fats.

Target: 35–50g protein

Option 1:

4 whole eggs (24g protein)

1 cup Greek yogurt (15–20g protein)

1–2 whole wheat rotis or oats

Handful of nuts

Water + electrolytes

Option 2:

Oats with milk

1 scoop whey protein mixed in (20–25g protein)

Peanut butter

2 boiled eggs

👉 Why: Eggs + dairy digest slowly and help reduce hunger during fasting.

🌇 Iftar (Breaking Fast) – Recovery Focus

Break fast light, then eat your main protein meal.

Step 1 (Immediate):

2–3 dates

Water

1 scoop whey protein (20–25g protein)

Step 2 (Main Meal after Maghrib):
Target 40–60g protein

150–200g chicken breast / fish / lean beef
(35–45g protein)

Rice or roti

Lentils (dal)

Salad

Yogurt

If vegetarian:

Paneer (150g = ~25–30g protein)

Lentils

Chickpeas

Soy chunks (very high protein)

🌙 Post-Workout (If You Train After Iftar)

1 scoop whey (20–25g protein)

Banana

Water

🌌 Before Bed (Optional but Powerful)

Slow-digesting protein helps overnight recovery.

Greek yogurt / cottage cheese / casein shake
(20–30g protein)

📊 Total Daily Protein Target

Depends on body weight:

For muscle gain: 1.6–2.2g per kg body weight

For fat loss: 1.8–2.4g per kg body weight

Example:
If you weigh 75 kg →
Aim for 120–165g protein per day.

💧 Hydration Tips (Very Important in Ramadan)

2.5–3.5 liters water between Iftar & Suhoor

Add electrolytes or a pinch of salt

Avoid too much fried food (causes dehydration)

⚠️ Common Mistakes in Ramadan

❌ Too many fried snacks at Iftar
❌ Not enough protein at Suhoor
❌ Skipping water
❌ Training intensely while dehydrated

If you tell me:

Your weight

Your goal (bulking / cutting / maintenance)

Veg or non-veg

I can make a custom Ramzan diet plan just for you.

1️⃣ Emphasize Lats for Width
The latissimus dorsi are the key “wings” that create that V shape.

Top Exercises for Width:

Pull-Ups / Chin-Ups (wide grip emphasizes lats)

Lat Pulldowns (wide grip, slow eccentric phase)

Straight-Arm Pulldowns (isolates lats without biceps takeover)

Tip: Think about pulling elbows down and back, not just using your hands.

2️⃣ Build Thickness with Mid-Back Work
A thick mid-back adds depth and separation.

Top Exercises for Thickness:

Barbell Rows / Dumbbell Rows (chest supported or bent-over)

Seated Cable Rows (close grip or V-bar)

T-Bar Rows

Tip: Keep your back straight and squeeze shoulder blades at the top of each rep.

3️⃣ Strengthen Rear Delts & Traps
Rear delts and traps create upper-back width and a more pronounced V-taper.

Exercises:

Face pulls

Reverse flys

Shrugs (traps)

4️⃣ Focus on Mind-Muscle Connection
Don’t just move the weight — feel your lats contracting.

Pause 1–2 seconds at the peak of each contraction.

5️⃣ Incorporate Progressive Overload
Increase weight or reps gradually

Mix rep ranges: 6–12 for strength, 12–20 for pump

6️⃣ Accessory Tips for V-Taper
Keep waist tight and core engaged — a smaller waist enhances the V shape

Maintain proper posture: shoulders back and chest out

Sample Back Day for V-Taper
Wide-Grip Pull-Ups: 4×8–12

Barbell Rows: 4×8–10

Seated Cable Rows (V-Bar): 3×10–12

Straight-Arm Pulldowns: 3×12–15

Face Pulls: 3×15–20

Dumbbell Shrugs: 3×12–15

I started my ICN prep journey on 27th November 2024, when my body weight was 64.4 kg. The journey was long and demanded patience, discipline, and consistency.

Over the first four months, I lost around 10 kg (by March). From April, I moved into a lean gain phase for the next four months, focusing on building quality muscle while staying controlled.

In August, I began training under my coach @yashsharma.fitness  That’s when we officially started the cut. At that time, my body weight was 56.6 kg. Over the next four months, I lost another 6–7 kg, bringing my stage weight down to 49.8 kg.

The goal was simple but challenging to reach single-digit body fat without losing muscle mass. This wasn’t a rushed transformation,it was a 12-month prep, built on structure, trust in the process, and countless disciplined days.

5 Fitness Facts Most People Don’t Know 🤯

Fitness isn’t just about working out harder — sometimes the small things make the biggest difference.

Here are a few interesting facts that might surprise you:

🔥 Your body can keep burning calories for hours after a workout.
🧠 Exercise doesn’t just strengthen your body — it improves brain function too.
🥶 Cold environments can slightly increase calorie burn.
🍽️ Eating slowly can help reduce overeating.
🦵 Your legs contain the largest muscles in your body.

Little habits like these can make a big difference over time.

5 Ways To Boost Your Metabolism 🔥

Your metabolism plays a big role in how many calories your body burns each day. While genetics play a part, your daily habits can also help support a healthy metabolism.

Here are 5 simple things that can help:

🥩 Eat More Protein
Protein requires more energy for your body to digest and can help keep you full for longer.

🏋️ Build Muscle
Muscle burns more calories than fat, even when you're resting.

🚶 Move More During The Day
Small activities like walking more, taking the stairs, and staying active all add up.

💧 Drink Enough Water
Hydration helps your body function efficiently and supports overall metabolism.

😴 Get Enough Sleep
Poor sleep can disrupt hormones that control hunger and energy levels.

Small daily habits can make a big difference over time.

🔥 Plank – strengthens your core and stability

Complete 2 rounds for a quick and effective fat loss workout.

3 Beginner Workout Mistakes ❌

If you're new to the gym, avoiding these common mistakes can help you get results faster and stay injury-free.

❌ Skipping the warm-up
Warming up prepares your muscles and joints, reducing the risk of injury and improving performance.

❌ Using too much weight
Focus on proper form first. Lifting too heavy too soon can lead to bad technique and injuries.

❌ No workout plan
Walking into the gym without a plan leads to wasted time and slower progress.

Consistency + proper technique = results.

Start simple, follow a plan, and focus on improving a little each week.

Yes fellow coaches, my chin needs to be tucked and my back could be straighter. I was watching my form in the mirror 😜

I resumed this movement a month ago after years of avoiding it following a relapse of an SI injury originally caused during high school football.

Roughly 5 years of on/off sciatic pain and muscles spasms. At its peak i could hardly hinge or drive a car without pain, let alone lift or run. I was wearing a back brace while seeing clients/patients and taking muscle relaxers and prescription strength anti-inflammatories. 

Today, no meds, no pain, and able to lift, run and compete how I want. My patience and mental health no longer being tested daily by the chronic pain and the inability to exercise. 

I became a victim of my own advice: AN INJURY IS MORE OF A LESSON THAN A SETBACK. The lesson was my training and recovery was completely out of balance and utterly lacked balance/stabilization/corrective exercise implementation. 

Incorporating more focus on recovery and utilizing stabilization and corrective exercise into every workout has completely rehabilitated something i was convinced was here to stay, also without surgery! It's good to be back! 😎💪

#225deadlift #sijointrehab #correctiveexercisespecialist #exercisetherapyspecialist #exercisemotivation

2012 Dallas Turkey Trot was the last time I ran this distance in one go, finishing in 67min. You can say my running journey started with this Turkey Trot, going from a recreational bodybuilder who wasn't running at all, to running 8miles within 6 months. Since 2012 I've run off/on while going in and out of various sports and training styles. 

Matching or beating my 67min initial time would've been nice but the goal today was to simply cover this distance at my easy/zone2 pace. Looking back, I had ZERO awareness in pacing, thresholds, form, cadence, and repeats in 2012. My strategy was simple: listen to fast-paced music and eat A LOT then hope for the best! Lol

Today running has become a weekly regimen since falling in love with DEKA and Hyrox a few years back. Until today my longest distance would be 10k, then threshold runs and repeats but never exceeding the 10k distance. 

The motivation to extend my distance is the upcoming Hyrox Houston for another Hyrox Solo Open AND a Pro Duo with @jorgelr2 on the same day! I'll be covering well over 10 miles between both races so this guy needs to up the dose on volume to prepare! LFG!!🏃💪

You don’t need to be perfect to make progress.

A lot of people quit their fitness journey because they think one “bad meal” ruins everything.

It doesn’t.

If you eat well 80% of the time, you’re still on track to:
💪 Build muscle
🔥 Lose fat
⚡ Improve energy

That’s:
✔️ 17 out of 21 meals each week
✔️ Consistency over perfection
✔️ Progress that actually lasts

The goal isn’t a “perfect diet.”
The goal is a sustainable healthy lifestyle you can stick to.

Enjoy the meal. Get back on track. Keep moving forward.

That’s how real fitness results happen.

How much protein do you really need?

A common guideline in the fitness world is:

About 1 gram of protein per pound of body weight.

This can help support:
💪 Muscle growth
🔥 Fat loss
⚡ Energy and recovery
🍽 Better appetite control

For example:
	•	150 lb person → ~150g protein per day
	•	180 lb person → ~180g protein per day
	•	200 lb person → ~200g protein per day

You don’t have to hit it perfectly every day, but aiming for a high-protein diet can make a big difference in your fitness progress and body composition.

Some easy protein sources:
🥚 Eggs
🍗 Chicken
🐟 Fish
🥩 Lean beef
🥛 Greek yogurt
🥤 Protein shakes
🌱 Beans and legumes

The real key is consistency over time.

Fitness isn’t random. It’s a process.

Too many people start a fitness journey without a clear plan and end up frustrated. Real progress comes from following a system.

Here’s a simple 4-phase fitness framework anyone can use:

Phase 1 – Plan
Define why your health and fitness matter. Decide what changes you want and create a realistic timeline.

Phase 2 – Test
Try your workout plan for about two weeks. Evaluate what’s working and adjust where needed.

Phase 3 – Work
Stay consistent with your training routine. Track improvements in strength, endurance, and agility while making small improvements.

Phase 4 – Results
Reach your goal, celebrate progress, and set the next challenge.

Fitness success isn’t about motivation.
It’s about structure, consistency, and discipline.

If you love pasta but still want to stay on track with your calories, this one is for you

Ingredients:
-250g Vetta Smart Protein Penne (instead of the whole packet)
-100g bacon
-1 brown onion, diced
-½ red capsicum, diced
-1 zucchini, diced
-150g baby spinach
-1 can Canned tomatoes
-250g Dolmio (about half the jar)
-60g Kalamata olives, sliced
-Salt, pepper, chilli flakes (optional)

Method
Cook pasta according to packet instructions. Drain and set aside.
Cook bacon in a large pan until lightly crispy.
Add onion and cook 2–3 minutes until softened.
Add capsicum and zucchini and cook another 4–5 minutes.
Stir in canned tomatoes and Dolmio sauce, simmer 8 minutes.
Add olives and spinach, cook until spinach wilts.
Toss through the cooked pasta and mix well.

Estimated Macros
(Recipe divided into 4 servings)

Per serve:
Calories: ~470 kcal
Protein: ~28–30 g
Carbs: ~55 g

Cardio isn’t just about burning calories, it’s about looking after your heart, lungs and overall health. ❤️

Whether it’s a run, cycle, walk, swim or row, regularly challenging your cardiovascular system improves endurance, heart health and energy levels.

But don’t forget the other side of the equation… strength training.
Lifting weights a few times per week helps build and maintain muscle, supports your metabolism and keeps your body strong and resilient.

And a reminder: cardio alone doesn’t cause fat loss.
Fat loss comes down to being in a calorie deficit over time. Exercise simply supports that process.

The sweet spot?
✔️ Regular cardio for heart health
✔️ Strength training to build muscle
✔️ Consistent nutrition to support your goals

Train your heart and your muscles. Your future self will thank you ☺️ 

Need help with an exercise plan? Learn how to track your macros, manage your intake and get results. I’d love to help you! 😄 

Send me a DM or drop a 𝙔𝙀𝙎 in the comments and I’ll forward you everything you need to know!

HARD TRUTH ⬇️ 

If you’re not losing weight over time, you’re most likely not in a calorie deficit.

No matter what type of exercise you choose to do, fat loss will always come back to a calorie deficit. Exercise can absolutely support fat loss, but it can’t override consistently eating above your calorie needs.

This is why tracking your calories matters. Without tracking, most people are simply guessing how much they’re eating.

Weight training is incredibly valuable because it helps build and maintain muscle, which can support a higher metabolism over time and help improve body composition.

And something as simple as daily walking is one of the most underrated tools for fat loss. Increasing your daily steps helps increase your overall energy expenditure without placing extra stress on the body.

So if your goal is fat loss:• Be consistent with your nutrition• Track your calories • Prioritise strength training• Keep your daily steps up

You don’t need to be perfect, you just need to be consistent. Consistency over time is what creates results. ❤️

Most people are structured during the week… but when the weekend comes, routines disappear and it’s easy to fall off track.

A little planning can make a huge difference.

Here are a few simple things you can do:

✔️ Write down the meals you’ll have for the weekend
✔️ Create a shopping list of exactly what you need
✔️ Go food shopping
✔️ Come home and prep what you can

Doing this will help you:
• Stay on track with your goals
• Save time when things get busy
• Save money at the supermarket
• Hit your macro targets more easily

A small amount of planning before the weekend can set you up for success instead of feeling like you need to “start again Monday”.

Save this post for your weekend reset routine. ✨

🥭 Tropical Protein Creami - 2 Serves 

Ingredients
1 cup frozen mango
1 and 1/2 passionfruit YoPRO pouch
3/4 cup milk of choice (I used unsweetened almond milk )

Method
1. Add mango, 1 x YoPRO yogurt pouch and milk to your Ninja Creami container and stir to combine.
2. Freeze overnight
3. Place the container into your Ninja Creami and select the Lite Ice Cream function.
4. Once spun, add 1 large tablespoon of extra yoghurt and press Respin. 
5. Continue adding 1 tablespoon of yoghurt at a time, respinning until smooth and creamy. (Mine needed 2 respins.)

Scoop and enjoy!

Macros per serve: 
Calories:  133
Protein: 15g
Carbs: 12:
Fat: 2g

Winter travels edition snacks and meals 💛

Cold weather and long ski days increase energy needs, so planning balanced meals and snacks is key.

I aim to keep meals structured using simple plate portions:
• ¼ carbohydrates for energy
• ¼ protein for muscle recovery
• ½ vegetables and fruit for fibre and micronutrients
• plus a source of healthy fats for satiety

For ski days, I pack practical snacks such as fruit, nuts, yogurt, and a sandwich to maintain steady energy between runs.

Balanced fueling supports recovery and endurance on the slopes 🏔️

Bowel cancer is no longer just a disease of older adults!

Recent research shows cases are rising in younger people and women, with early-onset bowel cancer increasing globally in adults under 50.
As a dietitian, I always remind my clients that lifestyle and nutrition play a key role in gut health. Supporting your colon health may include:
🥦 Eating more fiber-rich foods
🍎 Increasing fruits and vegetables
🌾 Choosing whole grains and legumes
🚶‍♀️ Staying physically active
🥩 Limiting processed meats and ultra-processed foods
Awareness, prevention, and early screening can save lives.
Listen to your body and don’t ignore symptoms.

Your gut health matters. 💙

#bowelcanceruk #bowelcancerawarenessmonth #colorectalcancerawarenessmonth #cancerprevention

Healthy Cookie Dough 🤍🍪 

Desserts can be both nourishing and satisfying. This high-protein cookie dough created with the most comforting ingredients is a simple and balanced option that works beautifully as breakfast or as a post-workout snack.

Recipe:
• 15 g vanilla protein powder
• 40 g almond flour from @marathasavalleynuts 
• 30 g plain yogurt (0–2%)
• ¼ tsp vanilla essence
• 5 g collagen type II powder
• 10 g dark chocolate chips @marathasavalleynuts 

Mix dry ingredients, add yogurt and vanilla, adjust texture if needed, fold in chocolate chips, and chill for 5–10 minutes.

Balanced. Practical. Dietitian-approved.

Tip: You can also bake the dough in the oven or air fryer!

STOP eating your meals in the wrong order! 🛑

Did you know that how you eat your food is just as important as what you eat? Science shows that eating your meal in this specific sequence can crush cravings and stop fat storage:

1️⃣ Fiber first (Veggies/Salad) 🥗
2️⃣ Protein & Fats second (Meat/Eggs/Beans) 🍗
3️⃣ Carbs & Sugars last (Rice/Bread/Dessert) 🍚

By the time the carbs hit your stomach, the fiber has already created a “protective mesh” that slows down sugar absorption. No spike, no crash, no extra fat storage. 🧠🔥

Will you try the 1-2-3 method today? Let me know in the comments! 👇

No flowers. No procession. No final goodbye.
Just quiet. Just absence. Just the ache.

And still… the grief came.

My dad became a donor to science.
It was a beautiful choice—but it left me with questions, too.
Would a traditional goodbye have helped? I’ll never know.

If you didn’t get a funeral, a service, or a goodbye—your grief is still real.
You still get to feel it. You still get to heal.

📝 Writing My Way Through Grief – a memoir on love, loss & letting go
🗓️ Releasing August 9

Stop Doing Squats Like This ❌

If you want better glute growth, stop making these common squat mistakes.

❌ Mistake 1: Knees collapsing inward

This reduces power and increases injury risk.

✔ Fix: Push your knees slightly outward during the movement.

❌ Mistake 2: Not going deep enough

Partial squats limit glute activation.

✔ Fix: Lower until your thighs are at least parallel to the ground.

❌ Mistake 3: Leaning too far forward

This shifts the focus away from your glutes.

✔ Fix: Keep your chest up and core tight.

🔥 Better squat form = better glute growth

After 12 years in the gym, here's EVERYTHING you need to build your dream body:
Run + Build Muscle

The chest, or pectoralis region, is primarily composed of two main muscles: the pectoralis major and the pectoralis minor. For training purposes, we typically divide the larger pectoralis major into three distinct functional areas based on the fiber direction.
​Here is a breakdown of the parts and the exercises that best target them:
​1. Upper Chest (Clavicular Head)
​These fibers originate near the collarbone and are best engaged by pressing movements that involve an upward angle.
​Exercises:
​Incline Dumbbell Press: Allows for a greater range of motion and better isolation.
​Incline Barbell Bench Press: Excellent for loading heavy weight.
​Low-to-High Cable Flys: Creates a great peak contraction.
​2. Middle Chest (Sternal Head)
​This is the largest part of the muscle, responsible for the main bulk of your chest. It responds best to flat, horizontal pressing movements.
​Exercises:
​Flat Bench Press (Barbell or Dumbbell): The gold standard for overall chest development.
​Flat Bench Dumbbell Flys: Great for stretching the muscle fibers.
​Push-ups: A versatile, classic movement for chest activation.
3. Lower Chest (Costal/Abdominal Head)
​These fibers originate from the ribs and are best targeted by pressing downward at a decline angle.
​Exercises:
​Decline Bench Press: Focuses the tension on the lower fibers while reducing strain on the shoulders.
​Dips (Chest-focused): Keep your elbows flared out slightly and lean your torso forward to emphasize the chest over the triceps.
​High-to-Low Cable Flys: Helps isolate the lower pectorals through constant tension.
​4. Inner Chest (Sternal Edge)
​While you cannot technically isolate the "inner" chest muscle fibers separately from the rest of the pectoralis major (muscle fibers run horizontally across the entire chest), you can maximize the contraction in the center of the chest.
​Exercises:
​Close-Grip Bench Press: Reduces the reliance on the outer chest and engages the sternal fibers more intensely.
​Cable Crossovers: Focusing on bringing your hands together and squeezing hard at the midline of your body.

☠️Legs = the biggest muscles in your body.
So stop training them blindly.

• If your legs recover fast → train 2×/week
• If they need 5–6 days to recover → train 1×/week

Training once a week?
👉 6–7 exercises are more than enough.
Training twice a week?
👉 4–5 exercises per session is enough.

And when training legs twice a week:
Day 1 → Quad-focused first
-Squats 3×5(heavy)
-Leg extension 3×12
-Walking Lunges 2×12
-Adductors +abductors 2×12 each
-Seated calf raise 3×15

Day 2 → Hamstring-focused first
-Leg press 3×10
-Leg curl 3×12
-Reverse lunges 3×10
-Goblet squats 3×12
-Standing calf raise 3×12

My Leg routine 👇
-Squats 3×5
-Reverse lunges(On stepper, smith machine)3×10
-Lying Leg curl 3×12
-Leg extension 3×12
-adductor + abductors 2 ×12 each
-Seated calf raise 3×12

If you’re trying to grow your glutes faster… read this twice 👇

Most people train hard, but they’re not training smart — and that’s why progress feels slow.

Here’s what actually makes the difference:

• Push through your heels → glutes take over (not your quads) 
• Slight toe turnout (10–20°) → better upper glute activation (“the shelf”) 
• Control your reps → explosive up, slow down, pause at the top 
• Start with heavy compounds → hip thrusts, RDLs, squats 
• Then finish with isolations → kickbacks, abductions 
• Fuel your workouts → carbs BEFORE = better performance + pump 
• Don’t ignore fiber → your body needs it to actually use protein

Simple tweaks… but they completely change your results.

Consistency + proper execution = growth. Every time.

Eat with your cycle, not against it 👇

Your body isn’t random — your hormones shift every week. 
And when your nutrition matches those phases… everything feels easier.

Here’s a simple breakdown:

🩸 Menstruation phase (low energy → replenish) 
Think: iron + omega-3 
Salmon, red meat, lentils, dark chocolate 
→ supports recovery, reduces fatigue

🌱 Follicular phase (energy rising → build) 
Think: light, nutrient-dense foods 
Eggs, kefir, kale, flaxseeds 
→ supports hormone production + gut health

🔥 Ovulation phase (peak energy → perform) 
Think: antioxidants + protein 
Quinoa, oysters, blueberries, broccoli 
→ supports performance + recovery

🌙 Luteal phase (cravings + fatigue → stabilize) 
Think: magnesium + slow carbs 
Sweet potato, banana, greek yogurt, pumpkin seeds 
→ helps with cravings, mood, and energy dips

This isn’t about being “perfect” — 
it’s about working with your body instead of fighting it.

This breakfast can give you over half your daily fiber needs in one meal, helping keep you full for hours and supporting sustainable weight loss goals.

Why I love it:
* It’s simple and actually tasty
* Keeps me full for hours
* Helps stabilize blood sugar
* Supports digestion (aka happy stools)

Overnight Chia Oat Parfait (18g fiber, 37g protein)

Ingredients:
* 2 tbsp chia seeds (8g fiber + 5g protein)
* ½ cup @bobsredmill Extra Thick Rolled Oats (4g fiber + 6g protein (extra thick oats are just better, trust me)
* ½ cup Greek yogurt (~13g protein, I used @chobani 20g protein no added sugar vanilla)
* 5 oz @fairlife 2% milk (13g protein)
*  ½ cup raspberries (4g fiber)

For reference, 18g fiber is already more than half the recommended daily intake for women, all in one breakfast.

Instructions: Add everything to a mason jar and stir until well combined. Let sit for ~3 minutes, then stir again so the chia seeds start expanding and don’t clump at the bottom. Repeat if needed. Seal and refrigerate overnight. Enjoy in the morning.

Simple, balanced, and actually satisfying.

What’s your go-to high-fiber breakfast? 👇

vegan dark chocolate strawberry brownies 🍫

follow 👉🏻 @healthyhelper and SAVE this recipe to make at home!!

these are adapted from my vegan #oreobrownies…made a little fruitier and a little FLUFFIER thanks to @smashfoods strawberry chia jam. they are still fully vegan, no added sugar, and they taste like the FUDGIEST dark chocolate brownie you’ve ever had. 🤤

-1 cup @bobsredmill gluten-free oat flour
-heaping 1/2 cup @eatsmashfoods strawberry chia jam
-¼ cup unsweetened black cocoa powder (this is KEY!!)
-½ tsp vanilla bean powder or extract
-1 tbsp baking powder
-½ tsp baking soda
-1/2-3/4 cup almond milk
-optional: 2 tbsp @nunaturals monkfruit sweetener (dc: healthyhelper), if you have a sweet tooth

instructions:
-preheat oven to 350. line an 8×8 baking dish with parchment paper or spray well with cooking spray.
-in a large bowl combine oat flour, cocoa, baking powder + baking soda, and vanilla bean powder.
-pour in jam and almond milk. mix together until evenly combined. let sit for at least 15 minutes for the chia seeds to become like “chia eggs” in the batter.
-spread brownie mixture into the prepared baking dish. bake for 18-19 minutes (do not overbake). remove from oven, let cool on a wire rack, slice, and serve with banana ice cream for a fully vegan dessert!

#healthybrownies #veganbrownies #veganbaking #nosugaradded

no-bake peanut butter pumpkin protein donut holes 🍩

follow 👉🏻 @healthyhelper and SAVE this recipe to make at home!

no bake, high protein, and seriously so good 🤝

these peanut butter pumpkin donut holes are packed with protein thanks to @ascent_protein vanilla bean whey…it adds the perfect touch of sweetness, a soft texture, and makes them actually filling without any baking.

they’re doughy, chocolate-studded, and come together in minutes… aka the easiest snack to keep stocked in your fridge all week.

made with simple ingredients like peanut powder, pumpkin butter, and coconut flour…but the real star is that clean, smooth @ascent_protein that makes the texture hit every time! 😮‍💨

ingredients:
-1/2 cup @ascent_protein vanilla bean whey protein powder
-3 tbsp @peanutbutterco peanut powder (dc: healthyhelper)
-6 tbsp @bobsredmill coconut flour
-1/2 cup @goodgoodbrand pumpkin butter (dc: healthyhelper)
-4 tbsp almond milk
-2 tbsp @letsbakebelieve chocolate chips

instructions:
-mix the whey protein, peanut powder, and coconut flour in a bowl.
-stir in the pumpkin butter and milk. stir until a thick dough forms.
-fold in the chocolate chips and stir again.
-roll into balls and refrigerate to firm up.

save this for your next sweet craving! ✨

was given 10 years to live after being diagnosed with terminal brain cancer. I decided that wasn’t the story I wanted to live by, so I went all in on my life from a holistic mindset.

What surprised me most along the way wasn’t the diagnosis. It was realizing how many people are trying to navigate life, hardship, and healing alone.

Community changes everything.

That’s why I created Here, For Life. A community led space where you can show up as you are. A place to share wins, hold yourself accountable, ask questions, and connect with others who understand what it means to face something hard.

I’ll be in there sharing what’s helped me along my journey, especially around diet, fitness, and mental health, BUT this isn’t about ME leading. It’s about US growing together.

This space is for finding comfort in the uncomfortable, learning through trial and error, and appreciating the small moments that make life worth showing up for.

Anything is welcome here. Breathwork, food ideas, focus practices, meditations, tools, or insights that have helped you live better.

If you’re craving connection, accountability, or a reminder that you’re not alone, I’d love for you to join us.

Life is too short to do it alone.

Join the Here, For Life community.

__________________
What helps people navigate hardship in life? How do you stay strong during hard times? Why is community important during hardships? How do people cope with illness and uncertainty? How do you find purpose through hardship? How do you keep going after a terminal illness diagnosis? What can brain cancer teach you about life? How do people heal mentally after illness? How do you stay motivated when life feels heavy? Why does community matter during illness and hardship? How do you stop feeling alone during hard seasons of life? How do you rebuild yourself after adversity? How do you grow through hardship instead of breaking? How do diet fitness and mental health support resilience? How do you find hope when life feels uncertain?

Life rarely happen in the zone of comfort. My biggest (positive) shifts in mindset, movement, and diet have always felt slightly uncomfortable. If you’re ready to grow, now…is always the time. ツ

The simplest way to make a healthy meal for your whole week! Or if you’re cooking for multiple people/ family.

1. You need a crockpot [it’s the easiest] (slow cooker, large pot, or Dutch oven works)
2. Two ingredients: a seasoning of your choice (ideally not salt). And high mineral salt (I use @redmondrealsalt)
3. Large pack of chicken - I do around 6 lbs which lasts me an entire week usually. (I do thighs but breast works)
4. Put everything in the crock pot together and mix it up. I typically just dump what I feel is necessary. But will go easy on the salt. You can always add more to taste when it’s almost done.
5. Set it and forget it. 5+ hours on high or 7+ hours on low. It’s almost impossible to overcook it. So you can go about your life and come back to it. 
(You’ll know it’s done because the meat will literally shred when you poke it)

👩‍🍳 This should realistically take you about 15 minutes all in and now you can feel good about what you’re eating for the rest of the week. Whether you’re on a budget or just hate cooking. This takes the work out of it. And tastes DELISHHHH

🍃I opt for organic and usually get everything at Costco, so each meal works out to about $2.50 or less per serving.

Stop talking down to yourself. 

I found that I when I would make a mistake I would call my self ‘dumb’ or ‘idiot’. Most of the time, It’s because I was in a rush. And my brain was moving faster than my body. 

It’s time to reframe that negative mindset. 
MISTAKES HAPPEN. Instead say “I’m human and it was a mistake”. Then move on. Don’t be hard on yourself. ☻ 

You’ve got this! And you are loved. 🫶🏼

What the heck is protein? 💪🏻

✨ A macronutrient with 4 calories per gram 
✨ It’s essential for muscle building & recovery 
✨ Complete proteins (e.g meat, fish and dairy products) should be prioritised 
✨ The amount of protein for each person needs each day can vary depending on multiple factors ~ however the recommended amount for most is 1.4-2.2g per kg of body weight per day 

Majority of people inside and outside of the gym hear the word “protein” however don’t necessarily understand what it is and why we need it. I hope this helps those have a better understanding ~ however feel free to send me a DM with any questions you may have 🫶🏻🥰🏋🏻‍♀️

#whattheheckisprotein #weneedit #protein #musclebuilding #recovery

✨ 2XU Triathlon Series 2026 ~ Race 4: Sandringham✨

750m 🏊🏻‍♀️ 20km 🚴‍♀️ 5km 🏃🏼‍♀️

Conditions =  warm, windy and sunny 💨 ☀️😎 

🔹 The swim = it’s been a while since I’ve done an open water swim so I was a bit nervous. But the water temperature was perfect, the water was nice and calm at the start but the further into the water we got the wind picked up. The water became very choppy and I definitely swallowed some water but I got through it fine just a bit slower than I would’ve liked 🥹

🔹 The bike = Started off really well, the course had more hills then I remember, which just meant I had to pump the legs extra hard. Once you made the halfway turn and started to head back you were riding against the wind which was very tough. After I got off the bike at transition the legs were very jelly 🥵

🔹 The run = Without a doubt the run is my strongest leg, but that doesn’t mean it’s not hard. By then your body is pretty fatigued and you’re just trying to keep moving so the body doesn’t cramp or stiffen up. I try to find a good pace I can maintain and only stop if I really need to or to have some water. Once we hit the 2.5k mark I was feeling good and confident about myself and able to cross that finish line strong 🏁💪🏻

Cardio Mistakes People Still Make ❌

Cardio isn’t the problem.
Bad strategy is.

Common mistakes:
• Doing cardio only to “burn off” food
• Going too hard, too often
• Ignoring strength training
• Thinking more sweat = more fat loss

Smart cardio supports your training.
It doesn’t replace it.

Balance it.
Program it.
Recover from it.

ข้อผิดพลาดเรื่องคาร์ดิโอที่ยังเจอกันบ่อย ❌

คาร์ดิโอไม่ใช่ปัญหา
แต่การใช้ผิดวิธีคือปัญหา

ข้อผิดพลาดที่พบบ่อย:
• ทำคาร์ดิโอเพื่อ “ล้างแคลอรี”
• ทำหนักเกินไปบ่อยเกินไป
• ไม่เล่นเวทเลย
• คิดว่าเหงื่อเยอะ = ไขมันลดมาก

คาร์ดิโอที่ดีควรเสริมการฝึก
ไม่ใช่แทนที่มัน

ทำอย่างสมดุล
วางแผนให้เหมาะ
พักฟื้นให้พอ

Leg Press Mistakes That Kill Your Gains ❌

The leg press isn’t useless.
But most people use it wrong.

Common mistakes:
• Half reps
• Locking knees hard at the top
• Ego loading too much weight
• Lifting hips off the seat

Fix it:
• Full controlled range
• Stop short of knee lockout
• Keep your lower back planted
• Control the negative

Load matters.
But execution matters more.

ข้อผิดพลาดในการเล่น Leg Press ❌

Leg Press ไม่ได้แย่
แต่หลายคนทำผิดวิธี

ข้อผิดพลาดที่พบบ่อย:
• เล่นไม่สุดระยะ
• ล็อกเข่าแรงเกินไป
• ใส่น้ำหนักมากเกินจำเป็น
• สะโพกยกออกจากเบาะ

วิธีแก้:
• เล่นเต็มระยะ คุมจังหวะ
• ไม่ต้องล็อกเข่าสุด
• หลังล่างแนบเบาะ
• คุมตอนลงช้า ๆ

น้ำหนักสำคัญ
แต่เทคนิคสำคัญกว่า

Lower with intent — don’t drop it.

Something I rarely discuss is my type 2 diabetes journey. Diagnosed in 2009 during a time of poor health and obesity, especially after 2 pregnancies that required insulin. Fast forward to my health and fitness transformation - I'm now diet-managed and have maintained non-diabetic ranges for 2 years! However, I've noticed that intense workouts can sometimes cause lows if my nutrition isn't spot on. Currently, I'm in a mini cut to get back on track to break some habits that were creeping back in and let me tell you, it's been an eye-opener. Reduced calories mean low mood, low energy, decreased gym strength, and poor stamina. It's a harsh reminder of how crucial nutrition is for fitness goals and overall well-being!

5:30 AM thoughts over açai bowls 🌅

People always ask about my morning routine, and honestly? It's not about perfection. Some mornings I'm up at 5 AM crushing it. Other mornings I hit snooze three times and stumble to the kitchen half-awake.

But what I TRY to do consistently:
✨ Hydrate first (water before coffee)
✨ Move my body (even if it's just stretching)
✨ Fuel intentionally (like this berry smoothie bowl)
✨ Set one intention for the day

The routine isn't about being Instagram-perfect. It's about creating anchors that support who you want to become.

What's one non-negotiable in your morning routine?

#morningroutine #wellnessjourney #healthyhabits #morningmotivation #mindfulmorning #wellnesslifestyle #healthylifestyle #morningritual #selfcareroutine #intentionalliving #smoothiebowl #morningvibes #wellnesswarrior #healthymindset #dailyroutine

The truth behind the highlight reel 💭✨

You see the workouts, the meal prep, the motivation. But can I be real with you for a second?

Some mornings I don't want to work out. Some days I eat pizza for dinner. Some weeks I skip the gym entirely because life gets overwhelming. And that's okay.

I'm not perfect. I'm not "on" 24/7. I'm just someone who decided that showing up imperfectly is better than not showing up at all.

The fitness journey isn't linear. It's messy. It's full of setbacks and comebacks. It's choosing yourself even when you don't feel motivated. It's progress over perfection, every single time.

So if you're feeling behind or like you're not doing enough—you are. You're here. You're trying. That's more than most people do.

Keep going. The journey is the destination.

What's one thing you need to hear today? Tell me below—I'm listening 💛👇

Friday Fuel: Protein Pancakes that actually taste amazing 🥞✨

Recipe incoming because you've been asking!

Protein Pancakes (makes 2 servings):
• 1 ripe banana
• 2 eggs
• 1 scoop vanilla protein powder
• 1/4 cup oats
• 1 tsp cinnamon
• Pinch of baking powder

Blend everything, cook on medium heat, flip when bubbles form. Top with berries, Greek yogurt, and a drizzle of almond butter.

Macro-friendly AND delicious? Yes, it's possible. 🙌

This keeps me satisfied until lunch and supports my training. No crash, no cravings, just sustained energy.

HYROX Auckland 2026!
20 members competed, an epic support crew in attendance, 8 flags coming back to Waihi Beach 🏆
Proud is an understatement 🙌🏼💪🏽

#Litorefitstudio #Hyrox #hyroxanz #hybridathlete #legendsportswear

One thing events like Hyrox tend to show people is this: You’re often more capable than you thought.

Not because something changed overnight, but because of all the steady sessions that came before it. The regular mornings. The small progressions. The days you showed up even when you didn’t feel like it.

That’s what builds confidence. Not one big moment, but lots of quiet ones adding up over time.

Watching our crew in Auckland was a reminder of that. What people can do when they’ve been supported, coached, and encouraged along the way.

Most people don’t start because they think they should already be fitter first.

They think they need more stamina, more strength, more confidence before walking through the door.

You don’t.

You can start exactly as you are.

No one here is expecting you to be anything other than someone who showed up. That’s always enough.

Just start.

It’s normal to feel a bit unsure before your first Spin class.

Most people wonder if they’ll be able to keep up, or if they’ll know how the bike works. 

We show you how to set everything up, explain the ride before we start, and you control your own resistance the whole way through.

You can ride easy, steady, or somewhere in between. No one’s measuring you. You just move at a level that feels right for you.

Starting is the only part that feels unfamiliar. The rest settles in pretty quickly.

 #litorefitstudio #waihibeachnz #spin #spinclass

Today’s Hyrox Sim. The crew turned up ready to work.

#litorefitstudio #litorefit #hyrox #hyroxtraining #hyroxnz

Support changes how training feels. Instead of wondering what to do next or whether you’re doing something right, you have someone guiding you, adjusting movements to suit you, and paying attention while you move, even in a group session.

That kind of support makes a real difference. It lets you focus on the work instead of the guesswork.

At Litore, every session is coached this way. You’re never left to figure things out on your own.

#litorefit #litorefitstudio #waihibeachnz #waihibeach #hiitclass #hiitworkout #hiitcardio

Motivation comes and goes. That’s normal.

Some days you feel ready to train. Other days you feel tired, busy, distracted, or just not quite in the mood. That doesn’t mean anything’s gone wrong. It just means you’re human.

What actually makes the difference over time isn’t how motivated you feel. It’s how often you show up, even when motivation isn’t there.

That’s why everything here is built around consistency. Structured sessions. Clear guidance. Support around you. So you don’t have to rely on willpower to keep going.

#waihibeachnz #waihibeach #hyrox #hyroxtrainingclub #hyroxtraining #hyroxtrainingclubnz #hyroxsimulation #litorefitstudio #litorefit

If you have been thinking about starting, this is a simple place to begin.

We meet at 6am at The Deli in Waihi Beach and head out together for a relaxed run. 

Most people do 30 minutes out and 30 minutes back, but you can go shorter or longer. It's completely up to you.

You do not need to be fast. You do not need to be experienced. You just need to turn up.

Afterwards, we usually head for a quick swim and a coffee before getting on with the day.

 #litorefitstudio #litorefit #waihibeach #waihibeachnz #run

Trying something structured like Hyrox can look a bit intimidating from the outside.

What most people find once they step in is that it’s actually very learnable when it’s coached properly and broken down at the right pace.

Litore is a Hyrox affiliated gym. That means our sessions follow the same training framework and movement style used in Hyrox events, but in a way that’s guided, adjustable, and suited to different experience levels.

Our Hyrox simulation classes aren’t about competing. They’re there to help you understand the format, practise pacing, and build confidence with it over time.

#waihibeach #litorefitstudio #litorefit #hyrox #hyroxtraining #HYROXTrainingClub #hyroxtrainingclub #hyroxtrainingclubnz #hyroxsim #hyroxsimulation

It’s not a tennis lesson, and it’s not about being good at tennis. 

It’s just movement that feels engaging, light, and a bit different from the usual.

You’ll move, react, laugh, adjust, and probably surprise yourself with what your body can do when you’re focused on responding and moving rather than overthinking it.

It’s a really easy way to get moving again if you’ve been feeling a bit out of routine, or if traditional workouts have never quite felt like your thing.

Just come along and give it a go.

Cosa farei se dovessi costruire i miei glutei da ZERO? 🍑

Smetterei di complicarmi la vita e mi concentrerei su questi punti:

1️⃣ Migliorerei la postura, ogni giorno
Un buon profilo gluteo parte da una buona postura.
Inserirei esercizi di stretching e mobilità quotidiani per anche, flessori, zona lombare.
Perché se il bacino è in antiversione o retroversione marcata i glutei non lavorano come dovrebbero.

2️⃣ Mangerei in lieve surplus calorico
Direi addio alle diete estremamente ipocaloriche.
Per costruire muscolo serve energia.
Sì, forse l’addome non sarà super piatto 365 giorni l’anno, ma se vuoi glutei pieni e sodi, devi mangiare abbastanza.

3️⃣ Pochi esercizi, fatti bene, per tanto tempo
Non cambierei scheda ogni 2 settimane.
Mi concentrerei su:
• Hip thrust
• Stacchi
• Affondi
• Slanci e abduzioni

E lavorerei sulla progressione dei carichi.
Più forza = più stimolo = più crescita.

4️⃣ Non farei tutto da sola
Mi farei seguire.
Avere una guida su allenamento e alimentazione è ciò che ti permette di:
– abbreviare le tempistiche
– evitare errori
– ottenere risultati veri, non casuali

I glutei wow non arrivano per caso.
Arrivano con strategia, costanza e metodo.

Se vuoi costruire anche tu la tua pesca 🍑
commenta PESCA o scrivimi in DM.

#gymtips #glutegains #gluteworkout #growglutes #allenamentoalfemminile

Chi ha glutei piatti spesso segue un’alimentazione troppo ipocalorica, con pasti poco bilanciati.
Magari si scelgono solo alimenti considerati “sani” come frutta e verdura, pensando che basti questo per migliorare il proprio fisico.

Così facendo però non dai al corpo l’energia e i nutrienti necessari per costruire muscolo.

Chi invece ha glutei tonici e pieni, nella maggior parte dei casi segue un’alimentazione:

✔ normocalorica (a volte anche con un lieve surplus)
✔ con pasti bilanciati in carboidrati, proteine e grassi
✔ non teme i carboidrati

Se vuoi costruire una 🍑 wow, devi mangiare meglio e in modo strategico.

Se hai spesso gambe gonfie, pesanti o infiammate, sappi una cosa:

l’allenamento da solo non basta.

L’alimentazione gioca un ruolo fondamentale nella gestione di infiammazione, ritenzione idrica e salute del microbiota.

Questo è un esempio di pasto semplice ma strategico:

🥝 Kiwi e mirtilli 
ricchi di vitamina C e polifenoli che aiutano a contrastare lo stress ossidativo e supportano il microcircolo.

🥛 Kefir 
fonte naturale di probiotici che migliorano la salute intestinale e contribuiscono a ridurre l’infiammazione sistemica.

🌾 Granola con avena 
ricca di beta-glucani che stabilizzano la glicemia e nutrono il microbiota.

Un intestino più sano significa spesso meno gonfiore e meno ritenzione.

Ma ricorda: 
nessun alimento da solo può fare miracoli.

Per questo in LT Program lavoriamo su tre pilastri:

allenamento + nutrizione + strategie drenanti.

Az egyik leggyakoribb kérdés, amit kapok:
Mit egyek este?

👉🏼 a jó hír: nem kell lemondanod az álomalakról akkor sem, ha este eszel vagy amikor étterembe mész - csak tudatosan kell választani.

Mire figyelj?
✔️ Könnyű, natúr étel legyen, néhány hozzávalóból
✔️ Magas fehérjetartalom, alacsony-közepes szénhidrát
✔️ Mérsékelt adag, ne érezd tőle magad teljesen tele
✔️ Lehetőleg este 7 óra előtt

Jó opció például a hal/csirke + grillezett zöldség vagy cottage cheese/tojás + saláta pl. olivás öntettel. Magasabb ch-tartalmú köret ki is hagyható, vagy mértékkel rizs vagy édesburgonya

Én garnélás pad thai-t választottam, ami rizstésztával készül, de a cézár saláta kenyér nélkül is jó választás lehet - és nem csalódtam🤭

Italként pedig a cukormentes verziókat vagy a vizet ajánlom.

Od začátku jsem věděl, že chci vytvořit víc než jen značku. Chtěl jsem vybudovat komunitu lidí, kteří se vzájemně podporují, motivují a posouvají dál. Každý z nás je jedinečný, máme různé cíle a cesty, ale spojuje nás stejná vize – být nejlepší verzí sebe sama. We’re unique.

Co jsi dnes udělal(a)pro lepší verzi sebe?

Většina lidí chce změnu. Lepší formu, více energie, více peněz. Ale jen málokdo si každý den opravdu položí jednoduchou otázku: co jsem pro to dnes udělal?

Ne zítra.
Ne od pondělí.
Dnes.

Často přitom přesně víme, co bychom měli dělat. Trénovat. Lépe jíst. Více spát. Méně prokrastinovat. Více pracovat na sobě.

Jenže místo toho často hledáme nový plán, lepší systém nebo další motivaci.

A možná tady přichází trochu kontroverzní myšlenka: většinou nepotřebujeme nový plán. Potřebujeme dělat ty základní věci dostatečně dlouho. ⚡

Pokud jste ještě nečetli knihu Atomové návyky, můžu ji jen doporučit. 📚 Jedna myšlenka z ní mi zůstala v hlavě: nezmění tě jedna velká změna, ale malé návyky, které opakuješ každý den. Každé rozhodnutí je malý hlas pro člověka, kterým se chceš stát.

Proto nemusíš změnit celý život ze dne na den. Stačí malé kroky. Jeden po druhém.

Ve výsledku je to možná jednodušší, než si chceme přiznat. Tvoje forma, energie i výsledky jsou jen součet toho, co děláš každý den… a toho, co opakovaně neděláš. 🧠

Co jsi dnes udělal(a) ty, aby byl zítřek o trochu lepší? 💭

#MSAesthetics #AtomicHabits #Discipline #Consistency #BetterEveryday

Squat vs Hip Thrust 🍑
Co je lepší cvik na zadek? Dřep… nebo hip thrust?

Pokud je cílem rozvoj glutes – zejména u bikini fitness – ve většině případů dává větší smysl hip thrust.

Proč?
Většina lidí totiž nemá problém ve výběru cviku.
Má problém v tom, že nedokáže zapojit sval, který chce trénovat.

Vidím to denně.
Holky dřepují, jedou výpady, bulhary, výstupy…
ale místo zadku cítí hlavně stehna.

Výsledek? Dominantní kvadricepsy, ale zadek zaostává.

A pak přijdou na hip thrust a najednou „zadek pálí“.

Není to náhoda.
Hip thrust je jednodušší na kontrolu pohybu, umožňuje silnou kontrakci a dává mnohem větší jistotu, že opravdu zapojuješ glutes.

Naopak dřep a další komplexní cviky jsou skvělé.
Ale pokud nemáš techniku, kontrolu a muscle-mind connection, velmi snadno z nich uděláš cviky na stehna.

A důležitá věc:
To, že u abdukcí (roznožování) cítíš zadek víc než u hip thrustu nebo výpadů,
neznamená, že ten cvik funguje líp.
Je to lehčí, izolovanější cvik.

„Pálení“ 🔥při tréninku není jediný ukazatel kvality.

Za mě má trénink jasnou logiku:
Komplexní cviky jako základ.
Izolace jako doplněk.

Ale pokud je cílem maximální rozvoj zadku, hip thrust by měl mít své pevné místo.

Dobře odcvičený dřep = skvělý cvik.
Dobře odcvičený hip thrust = skvělý cvik.

Ale pokud si mám vybrat jeden pro glutes?
Hip thrust.

A ano – platí to nejen pro bikini závodnice,
ale klidně i pro chlapy. 💪

Cítíš víc zadek… nebo stehna? 👇

Pro závodníky.🗯️

Někdy se v přípravě může stát jedna věc.
Postupně začneš ztrácet sám sebe.

Člověk je zahleděný do cíle, do formy, do výsledku…
a často si toho ani nevšimne.

Ale jeho okolí ano.

Někdy je to jen obrovský zápal pro věc.
Někdy tlak, únava a vyčerpání.

A někdy do toho vstupují i hormony nebo jiné věci, které dokážou změnit náladu i vnímání reality.

Je to hlubší téma, než se na první pohled zdá.

Píšu to i proto, že jsem se s tím osobně setkal. Viděl jsem to u lidí kolem sebe.

A v určité fázi života jsem kvůli tomu přišel i o vztah.

Tenhle sport dokáže člověka pohltit víc, než si někdy připouštíme. A někdy za to člověk zaplatí víc, než si uvědomuje.

Někdo v tom období ztratí přátele.
Někdo obětuje vztah.

Až zpětně si uvědomí, jak moc ho ten kolotoč pohltil.

Tenhle sport je nádherný.

Ale forma, medaile ani stage by nikdy neměly vzít to nejdůležitější.

Tebe samotného.

Zůstaňte sami sebou. 💪

Volby prezidenta Svazu kulturistiky ČR 🇨🇿

Bohužel jsem se letošních voleb osobně nemohl zúčastnit a vyslechnout si vše, co se tam řešilo.
Podle informací z různých stran to ale byla poměrně divoká diskuze.

Náš oddíl MS Aesthetics zastupovala Káťa a jsem rád, že měla možnost slyšet věci, do kterých já osobně už několik let částečně vidím a o které se snažím zajímat.

Ne kvůli politice.
Ale kvůli dobru české kulturistiky a hlavně našich závodníků a reprezentantů. 💪

Je totiž stále spousta věcí, které mají prostor ke zlepšení.
A tohle je jen malá část témat – celkové zázemí a podpora české kulturistiky může být ještě mnohem větší.

1. Podpora reprezentantů
Velkým tématem je financování reprezentace.

Cílem by podle mě mělo být, aby nominace byla přísnější, reprezentace byla skutečně výběrem toho nejlepšího a tito závodníci měli maximální možnou podporu při odjezdu na ME nebo MS. 🌍

V některých případech mám stále pocit, že převažuje kvantita nad kvalitou.

Přitom reprezentovat svou zemi by měla být čest i závazek. 🇨🇿

2. Tanning servis
Další věcí je organizace tanning servisu.

Je potřeba, aby nevznikaly dlouhé fronty a barva měla dostatek času správně zaschnout.

Pomoci by mohla:
• jasně daná časová okénka pro závodníky
• možnost volby aplikace u muže nebo ženy
• spolehlivý backstage servis pro korekce barvy a olej
• sjednocená cenová politika

3. Rozhodování
Rozhodování by mělo být co nejvíce sjednocené a konzistentní, aby závodníci věděli, podle jakých kritérií jsou hodnoceni.

Důležitá je také empatie k závodníkům, kteří do přípravy investují měsíce práce.

Velkým přínosem by byl i jasnější feedback, který by závodníkům pomohl pochopit výsledky a posunout se dál. 📊

Transparentnost a jednotný přístup totiž dlouhodobě posilují důvěru ve sport.

Já osobně věřím, že všechny tyto věci mají jeden společný cíl:

posouvat českou kulturistiku dál
a vytvářet lepší podmínky pro závodníky, kteří ji reprezentují. 💪

Ranní trénink nalačno. Ano nebo ne? 🤔

Téma, které se ve fitness řeší pořád dokola.
A často to vypadá, jako by existovala jen jedna správná odpověď.

Realita je ale trochu složitější.

Za roky coachingu jsem se setkal s oběma přístupy nesčetněkrát.

Někomu ranní trénink nalačno vyhovuje.
Cítí se lehčí, lépe se soustředí a trénink probíhá bez problémů.

U jiných je to přesný opak.
Bez sacharidů chybí energie a výkon jde dolů. ⚡

Po noci přichází tělo do tréninku s nižší hladinou glykogenu.
To může být v některých situacích výhoda, ale zároveň může chybět energie pro intenzivní výkon.

A otázka, kterou řeší hodně lidí:
Má trénink nalačno vliv na svalový růst?

Ve většině případů ne tak dramaticky, jak si lidé myslí.

Samotný trénink nalačno automaticky neznamená ztrátu svalů ani horší progres.

Mnohem důležitější je to, co sníš během dne 
celkový příjem energie, bílkovin a dlouhodobá konzistence.

Já osobně to mám jednoduché.
Pokud jdu trénovat do hodiny až hodiny a půl od probuzení, většinou nesnídám.
Spíš si hlídám, abych byl dobře najedený večer předtím.
Pokud je mezi probuzením a tréninkem víc času, snídani si dám.

A zajímavá věc z praxe:

Ne každý, kdo „potřebuje snídani před tréninkem“, ji potřebuje kvůli tělu.

Někdy ji potřebuje spíš hlava. 🧠

Na druhou stranu ale platí, že kvalitní výkon potřebuje energii.
Pokud chceš trénovat tvrdě a progresivně přidávat, tělo musí mít z čeho brát.

Proto neexistuje univerzální odpověď.

Za mě jako trenéra jsou klíčové dvě věci:
individualita a konzistence.

Protože výsledky nevytváří jedna snídaně před tréninkem.

Ale to, co děláš opakovaně každý den. 💪

Jak to máš ty?
Trénink ráno nalačno,
nebo se snídaní před tréninkem? 👇

#MSAesthetics #MorningTraining #FitnessNutrition #TrainSmart #ConsistencyWins

Upřímnost není vždy příjemná. Ale často je potřebná.

Jsem člověk, který říká věci narovinu.

Ne proto, že bych chtěl někoho kritizovat nebo shazovat. Ale protože věřím, že otevřená zpětná vazba posouvá věci dopředu.

Ať už ve sportu, v práci nebo v běžném životě.

Často ale vidím jednu věc.
To, co jeden člověk myslí jako snahu něco zlepšit, může jiný vnímat jako útok.

A někdy stačí, aby někdo slyšel jen část konverzace, vytrženou z kontextu.

Jenže mezi kritikou a zpětnou vazbou je velký rozdíl.

Kritika většinou hledá chybu.
Zpětná vazba hledá zlepšení.

A pokud je řečená s respektem a dobrým úmyslem, může být jednou z nejcennějších věcí, které můžeš dostat.

Ve sportu to vidím pořád.
Jsou lidé, kteří se při první “kritice” urazí.
A pak jsou lidé, kteří se na chvíli zamyslí a řeknou si:

„Možná na tom něco je.“
A právě ti druzí se většinou posouvají nejdál.

Někdy totiž nemluvíš jen za sebe.
Mluvíš i za lidi kolem sebe.

Denně trávím hodiny ve fitku se svými klienty a mluvím i s ostatními lidmi, kteří tam tráví spoustu času.

A právě od nich často slyším různé postřehy, nápady nebo věci, které by šly zlepšit.

Ne kvůli kritice.
Ale kvůli tomu, aby prostředí fungovalo co nejlépe pro všechny.

Proto jsem tyhle věci otevřel a snažil se bavit o procesech, fungování a možnostech, jak některé věci posunout dál.

A i když se na všem nemusíme vždy shodnout, věřím, že otevřená komunikace má smysl.

Protože ego chrání naše pocity.
Ale upřímná zpětná vazba může posunout věci dopředu. 🧠

Jak to máš ty?
Bereš zpětnou vazbu jako útok…
nebo jako příležitost něco zlepšit? 💭

To, co jednou stačilo na titul, nemusí za dva roky stačit ani na medaili.

A přesně to je realita soutěžního sportu.

V roce 2023 Péťa vyhrála Mistrovství Čech a následně se stala mistryní České republiky. 🥇

Pro spoustu lidí by to byl vrchol.

Ale v soutěžní kulturistice je to často jen začátek další práce.
Protože po každé sezóně přichází ta nejdůležitější fáze – feedback od rozhodčích a následná práce v off-seasonu.

Co bylo dobré.
Co rozhodčí viděli jinak.
A kam se musí postava posunout dál.

U Péti byl tehdy směr poměrně jasný:

→ širší záda
→ lepší rozvoj gluteální oblasti

Tak jsme zavřeli sezónu a začala práce v off-seasonu.

Off-season v bikini fitness není období, kdy se „čeká na další závody“.

Je to období, kdy se staví nová verze postavy.
Kdy se pracuje na detailech, které na pódiu rozhodují.

Více svalů.
Lepší proporce.
Lepší symetrie.
A hlavně trpělivost.

Protože změny v této kategorii často trvají roky, ne měsíce.

Právě tady se navíc ukáže,
kdo maká celý rok
a kdo jen tři měsíce před závody.

Po dvou letech práce se Péťa vrátila na pódium v roce 2025.

Nejlepší výsledek přišel na mezinárodní soutěži Mr. Universe – 6. místo. 🌍

Na domácí scéně to ale tentokrát na medaili nestačilo.

A to je další realita soutěžní kulturistiky.
Kategorie se neustále vyvíjí.

To, co bylo ideál před dvěma lety, dnes může být jen standard.

Konkurence roste.
Postavy se mění.
A nároky rozhodčích také.

Další směr práce je proto znovu jasný:

→ větší stehna
→ výraznější gluteus medius
→ širší ramena

Jako trenér ale jednu věc vnímám ještě víc než samotné umístění.

Progres.

Péťa každou sezónu přináší lepší verzi své postavy.
A právě to je v soutěžním sportu to nejdůležitější.

Protože umístění je výsledek jednoho dne.
Ale progres je výsledek let práce.

A přesně ten Péťa ukazuje každou sezónu. 🔥

Condivido con chi mi segue solo il mio lavoro, ma ho deciso di presentarmi un po’.

Sui social generalmente si mostra solo la perfezione… io non sono perfetta (anzi!).
Non ho superpoteri, né una super-forza, una super-elasticità, una super-resistenza o una super-altezza… insomma, sono perfettamente imperfetta — e va bene così!

Fin da ragazzina ho ammirato chi, nella vita, nel bene o nel male, anche quando sembra crollare il mondo, riesce comunque a spostarsi un po’ più in là.
Io ogni tanto perdo lo zen e mi faccio trasportare dalle passioni, ma ritrovo il mio equilibrio attraverso il movimento.

La carta d’identità dice che ho 50 anni… ma credo si sbagli! 😉

Non sono single, quindi incastrare tutto e riuscire a dare la giusta attenzione a tutti non è sempre semplice.

Molti pensano che io sia “solo” un’istruttrice fitness… in realtà sono anche un’istruttrice fitness, ma prima di tutto sono una professoressa di Filosofia e Storia, con mille interessi e tante passioni.
👉 Mens sana in corpore sano.

Ecco: sono esattamente come tutti voi.
Una vita incasinata che cerco di gestire al meglio, destreggiandomi tra doveri, piaceri e imprevisti, tenendo sempre pronti piani B, C e D.

Non faccio magie… ma posso aiutarvi a trovare e mantenere il vostro benessere psicofisico attraverso il movimento.

Ogni giorno trovate tante proposte di allenamento gratuite:
dalla ginnastica con o senza attrezzi al trampolino, dall’indoor cycling allo yoga.

👉 Seguitemi sul mio canale YouTube:
https://www.youtube.com/@MartaFovana

e sui miei social.

Non si parte per Roma senza conoscere la strada.
Eppure nel fitness molti credono che ci si possa allenare a casaccio e ottenere comunque ottimi risultati.

Si cercano scorciatoie,
ci si affida a promesse veloci,
ci si illude di raggiungere risultati facili.

Questo non è il mio pensiero.

Per me allenarsi è metodo, studio, esperienza.
È scienza, non opinione.

Significa accettare che non esistono scorciatoie,
che i risultati arrivano con impegno, costanza e tempo.

Ma allenarsi significa anche altro:
mettere la salute prima dello specchio,
lavorare seriamente senza perdere il sorriso,
perché la disciplina non deve togliere leggerezza.

Essere in forma non è un traguardo,
è uno stile di vita.

Preferisco i fatti alle parole.

Questo è il mio pensiero.
Se è anche il tuo, allenati con me.

LA SALUTE NON DEVE ESSERE UN PRIVILEGIO.

Il movimento non è riservato
a chi ha tempo infinito,
un corpo “perfetto”,
l’età “giusta”
o un talento naturale.

Il movimento è per chi sceglie.
Per chi decide di iniziare.
Per chi cade e riparte.
Per chi vuole stare bene — davvero.

Non prometto miracoli.
Offro strumenti.
Metodo.
Costanza.
Possibilità.

➡️Indoor cycling per migliorare la circolazione e rafforzare il sistema cardiovascolare.
➡️Ginnastica per rinforzare la muscolatura e sostenere forza e autonomia nel tempo.
➡️Trampolino per stimolare la densità ossea e favorire il sistema linfatico.
➡️Yoga per allungamento muscolare, mobilità articolare ed equilibrio.

Tante opportunità.
Un solo obiettivo:

STARE BENE.

Il movimento è un diritto.
E io lavoro ogni giorno perché sia accessibile a tutti.

✨ La live di ieri sera è stata … spiritosa 🍸🍸🍸 ad un certo punto si è parlato di alcolici.
Qualcuno potrebbe aver pensato che mi fossi 'bevuta il cervello', in verita mi sono lasciata trascinare da un bellissimo ricordo di una mia esperienza passata!

📍 Correva l’anno 2003 e io sono entrata nel Guinness dei Primati per il numero di cocktails presenti in listino e preparati in una sera al bar che al tempo gestivo

Fa quasi sorridere pensarci oggi, mentre parlo di movimento, benessere, equilibrio… Ma in verità quello che mi ha portato lì ieri è lo stesso che mi porta oggi qui:
➡️la curiosità
➡️ la passione
➡️la voglia di sperimentare
➡️il mettersi in gioco senza sapere già tutto

Dietro a ogni percorso, anche quello che sembra “fuori strada”, c’è sempre qualcosa che resta.

​🏠 Dal grande schermo al nostro CSE: quando l'amicizia si fa reale! ✨

​Oggi il nostro CSE Siwan brilla di una luce speciale: avere qui con noi Marina e Alberto è gioia pura! ✨ Non sono solo "visite", ma la prova concreta che lo sport può abbattere ogni muro e accorciare ogni distanza.

​🚴‍♂️ Tutto è iniziato con un "Play"
​Tutto è partito dalle lezioni di Marta Fovana. Dalla sua palestra in Piemonte, Marta ha il superpotere di bucare lo schermo delle sue live di Indoor Cycling: la sua voce e la sua energia arrivano dritte nei nostri cuori, rendendo lo sport un’esperienza davvero "pro-abile" e aperta a tutti. Pedalata dopo pedalata, quel monitor non è stato più un limite, ma un ponte.

​🇮🇹 Il magico ricordo di Bobbio
​Come non emozionarsi ripensando alla Convention di Bobbio? Due giorni indimenticabili dove noi del CSE Siwan e del Progetto D.D.N.ci siamo immersi in un mare di energia, musica, pranzi condivisi e nuovi incontri da tutta Italia. È lì che quella connessione nata online si è trasformata in qualcosa di profondo.

​🤝 Un legame che resta
​L’amicizia con Marina è nata nel ritmo della pedalata ed è cresciuta nel tempo, diventando oggi una presenza costante e affettuosa. Grazie di cuore a Marina e Alberto per i doni bellissimi, ma soprattutto per la vostra empatia concreta: la vostra presenza qui dà un valore reale a tutto quello che facciamo.
​Grazie Marta per aver reso possibile tutto questo. Lo sport unisce, emoziona e, soprattutto, crea legami che restano per sempre. 💖

Sheet Pan Dinner 🤍✨

One of our all-time favorites!! Pick your favorite protein, carb, and add some color with veggies!!

INGREDIENTS
~ TJ’s Unexpected Cheddar Chicken Sausage
~ Yellow Baby Potatoes
~ Asparagus
~ Bell Peppers
~ Yellow Onion
~ Olive Oil
~ Salt & Pepper
~ Balsamic Glaze

DIRECTIONS
1. Preheat oven to 425°F.

2. Dice the potatoes and season with olive oil, salt, and pepper. Spread on a sheet pan and bake for 20 minutes.

3. Dice the bell peppers and onions. Remove stems from the asparagus. Season veggies with olive oil, salt, and pepper. Dice the chicken sausage and add everything to the sheet pan. Bake for an additional 10–15 minutes.

4. Plate and drizzle with balsamic glaze if desired.

The BEST ever cinnamon rolls 🤎✨

Soft, fluffy, perfectly gooey centers with that brown sugar + cinnamon swirl… and the dreamiest cream cheese icing on top.

Pro tip: pour the butter + brown sugar + heavy cream mixture in the bottom of the pan for ultra caramelized, bakery-style rolls. You’ll never go back.

Save this one for holidays, brunches, or when you just need a cozy baking day 🫶✨

🤎INGREDIENTS🤎

Dough
150g (¾ cup) warm milk
1 packet instant yeast
¼ cup sugar
1 egg + 1 egg yolk
¼ cup melted butter
3 cups bread flour
¾ tsp salt

Filling
⅔ cup brown sugar
1½ tbsp cinnamon
⅓ cup softened butter

For the Bottom of the Pan
½ cup melted butter
½ cup brown sugar
¼ cup heavy cream
1 tsp vanilla extract
¼ tsp salt

Icing
4 oz cream cheese
3 tbsp butter
½ tsp vanilla
¾ cup powdered sugar
***Optional: Add orange zest/juice for orange cinnamon rolls 🍊

🤎DIRECTIONS🤎
1. Warm the milk on the stove or microwave until about 100°F. (Too hot will kill the yeast, too cold and it won’t activate.)

2. Add 1 tbsp of the sugar and the yeast to the milk. Let sit and activate.

3. Once activated, add the remaining sugar, egg, egg yolk, and melted butter.

4.  Add flour and salt. Knead (by hand or mixer) for 8–10 minutes until smooth.

5. Let dough rest in a bowl (glass is best) for 1 hour or until doubled in size.

6. Roll dough out to about ¼ inch thick (or to the size of your pan).

7. Spread softened butter over dough, then evenly cover with brown sugar and cinnamon mixture.

8. Roll the dough tightly into a log and slice with twine or use a pizza cutter to cut and roll individually.

9. Pour the butter/brown sugar/heavy cream/vanilla/salt mixture into the bottom of your baking dish.

10. Place rolls on top of the mixture. Let rise again until doubled, about 30–45 minutes.

11. Bake at 350°F for 22–27 minutes.

ICING 
12. Cream butter and cream cheese until smooth.

13. Mix in vanilla.

14. Slowly add powdered sugar.

15. Add heavy cream if needed to thin.

Spread over warm rolls and enjoy 🤍✨

The easiest and most delicious granola recipe you will try, and it comes together so quickly! 

Ingredients: 
-70g chopped almonds 
-200g oats 
-50g desiccated coconut 
-120g peanut butter 
-100g honey
-60g dark chocolate (optional) 

Combine all the ingredients ingredients (except the chocolate) in a bowl with some cinnamon, ginger, and salt, and mix until it is all combined. 
Then spread the mixture out in a lined baking sheet and bake at 160 degrees C / gas mark 3 for around 20 minutes. 
Stir or shuffle the granola around about halfway to stop it from burn in at the edges - if you want clumpy granola than flatten it back down after! 
Once cooled, add in your chocolate and store in an airtight container such as a jar. 

Enjoy !!

March Challenge 5 - Scheduling

- Keep up with “Daily 10min breaks” this week.
- Who has been there before: “Just one more...”, “I’m nearly done”.
- There isn’t enough time in my day...

The Plan:

- Set a clear finish time and stick with it!
- 15 minutes before finish time: Note all unfinished tasks, rank their priorities and schedule for the next day.

Your abs are already there.

Here’s how Hany Rambod coaches Andrei Deiu to build rounder, wider, 3D shoulders 👇

1️⃣ Drop the weight

Your delts don’t care how heavy the dumbbells are — they care about tension.
Going lighter helps you control the movement and keep the stress exactly where it should be: on the side delt, not the traps.

2️⃣ Lead with the elbows

Think of lifting out to the sides, not up.
Raising the elbows slightly higher than the hands keeps tension on the lateral head and reduces trap takeover.

3️⃣ Slight forward lean

A small lean makes the side delt the prime mover, increases the stretch, and gives you a cleaner line of pull.
Hany teaches this to almost every athlete using his FST-7 system.

4️⃣ Slow and controlled reps

The side delt responds best to controlled tempo, especially on the lowering phase.
If you’re swinging the weight, you’re losing gains.

5️⃣ High reps for growth

Hany’s signature style:
12–15 reps, 7 intense sets, short rest.
This pumps so much blood into the muscle that the delt has no choice but to grow.

⭐ Takeaway

You don’t need heavy weights for big shoulders.
You need precision, tension, and control — exactly how Hany teaches it.

Save this so you remember on your next shoulder day 💛⚡

Starting in your 30s, the body gradually begins to lose skeletal muscle mass, a process known as sarcopenia.

Research shows adults lose roughly 3–8% of muscle mass per decade, and the rate increases significantly after age 50.

By the time many people reach their 70s, they may have lost 30–40% of their muscle tissue.

This matters because muscle plays a critical role in:

• glucose regulation
• metabolic health
• balance and mobility
• longevity

Low muscle mass is strongly associated with increased risk of falls, metabolic disease, frailty, and early mortality.

The most effective way to slow or prevent this process is resistance training.

Strength training provides the mechanical stimulus needed to preserve muscle tissue and maintain metabolic health as we age.

Muscle isn’t just about aesthetics.
It’s long-term survival.

Mitchell et al., 2012 — Sarcopenia, Dynapenia, and the Impact of Advancing Age on Human Skeletal Muscle Size and Strength
Janssen et al., 2000 — Skeletal Muscle Mass and Distribution in 468 Men and Women

Skipping leg day might feel harmless, but it affects more than just your lower body.

Large compound leg exercises like squats, lunges, and deadlifts recruit the biggest muscle groups in the body. 

Because of this, they create a strong systemic training stimulus that increases overall muscle activation, metabolic demand, and anabolic signaling.

Training large muscle groups also leads to higher levels of mechanical tension and motor unit recruitment, which are key drivers of muscle growth.

In other words, leg training doesn’t just build your legs, it improves your overall training stimulus, helping the entire body adapt and grow.

So skipping leg day doesn’t just leave you with small legs.
It can also limit your overall progress.

Most injuries in the gym happen before the first working set because people skip a proper warm-up.

A good warm-up improves:

• joint lubrication
• muscle activation
• force production

Research shows warm-up sets increase performance and neuromuscular readiness, allowing you to lift heavier with better control.

The goal is simple:

1️⃣ Raise body temperature
2️⃣ Activate joints and muscles
3️⃣ Gradually increase load

This prepares your nervous system for maximum mechanical tension.
And mechanical tension is the primary driver of muscle growth.

Train smarter. 
Save this so you never skip your warm-up again.

my easy + comforting beef stew! 🥘 perfect for your luteal and menstrual phases. this @crockpot recipe is honestly so warm and nourishing- great for these last few rainy weeks in pdx! ☔️

here’s the run down: 
- 1 sweet onion 
- lots of chopped fresh garlic 
- 1 celery stalk 
- 1 pound yellow potatoes 
- big bag of green beans 
- 12-ish carrot sticks 
- 2 containers of organic beef stock (nice to have a 3rd for a few days in of eating, I keep refilling the crockpot when needed) 
- pound + a half of grass fed stew meat from the butchers counter 
- evoo, salt + pepper, paprika, italian seasoning, onion + garlic powder, crushed chili flakes 

it’s really just a matter of prepping your veggies and then adding it all to the crockpot with the browned meat! honestly took me 20 minutes to assemble and will keep in the fridge all week! 

#beefstew #hormonehealth #easydinners #healthymealprep

@fineartfruit is an utterly delicious NW stop for one-of-a-kind statement pieces, gifts for friends, unique home goods and so much more 🍒🍊🎨

greeted by the beautiful spanish colonial architecture, the shop lives inside the old paramount pictures film exchange building! 

built in 1935, the property was modeled after studio buildings in hollywood and allowed local theater managers to screen and rent films, making it a crucial distribution node in the pnw 🎥

the hallowed halls make @fineartfruit feel all the more surreal! colorful pieces and whimsical decor evoke a special kind of nostalgia. girlhood personified. the artful combination of fish, fruit and fashion is unlike anything else I’ve seen in portland, and certainly brings a smile to your face immediately upon entering 🐟

grab a latte + bite @princecoffeepdx then spend the afternoon basking in all the fashion wonder @fineartfruit has to offer! she is truly a decadent delight 🍇

VR Goggles on the Bench? The New Reality of Iron 🥽🦾

It’s 2026, and VR isn't just for gaming. It has entered the squat rack. High-performance athletes are now using AR/VR overlays to perfect their biomechanics in real-time.

• Visual Pathing: The goggles project the ideal bar-path in your field of vision, helping you correct your form mid-rep.
• Gamified PRs: Imagine seeing a digital ghost of your previous personal best lifting next to you. It’s the ultimate motivation hack.
• Data Overlay: Your heart rate, velocity, and power output are displayed right in front of your eyes.

Would you train in a virtual world if it meant perfect form, or is "old school" feel the only way? ⬇️🦾 #PowerliftingTips #FitnessLongevity #SmartTraining #BodyGoals #FitnessInspiration #FitnessNutrition #YogaInspiration #Mindfulness #AestheticFitness #BodyDefinition #StretchingRoutine #PowerliftingTechnique #FitnessEducation #Hypertrophy #MuscleScience #BodyBuildingTips #BioMechanics #TrainSmart

Hormesis: The Science of Radical Recovery 🧊🔥

In 2026, we don't just "rest." We use Hormesis—controlled biological stress to trigger cellular repair. 

• The Contrast: Moving from 90°C heat to 3°C cold shocks the system, forcing your mitochondria to produce massive amounts of energy for repair.
• Longevity: This "thermal stress" clears out dead cells (autophagy) and strengthens the heart better than steady cardio.
• Mental Grip: Staying calm in the ice is the ultimate discipline for your nervous system.

Are you a fan of the "shock" recovery, or do you prefer to keep it warm and comfortable? ⬇️🧐 #PowerliftingTips #FitnessLongevity #SmartTraining #BodyGoals #FitnessInspiration #FitnessNutrition #YogaInspiration #Mindfulness #AestheticFitness #BodyDefinition #StretchingRoutine #PowerliftingTechnique #FitnessEducation #Hypertrophy #MuscleScience #BodyBuildingTips #BioMechanics #TrainSmart

Ditch the Watch: The Smart Ring Era is Here 💍📈

The era of massive, distracting fitness watches is officially over. In 2026, the real flex is invisible data. Why are all the top lifters switching to Smart Rings?

• HRV is King: Medical-grade Heart Rate Variability (HRV) data lets you know if you are fully recovered and ready for a PR before you even step into the gym.
• Barbell Friendly: No more watch digging into your wrist during a heavy clean or a push press. Total grip freedom.
• Zero Distractions: No screens to distract you mid-set. Just pure, silent data flowing to your phone.

Are you still relying on your huge watch, or have you upgraded to the silent power of the ring? 👇✨ #PowerliftingTips #FitnessLongevity #SmartTraining #BodyGoals #FitnessInspiration #FitnessNutrition #YogaInspiration #Mindfulness #AestheticFitness #BodyDefinition #StretchingRoutine #PowerliftingTechnique #FitnessEducation #Hypertrophy #MuscleScience #BodyBuildingTips #BioMechanics #TrainSmart

Functional Beast Mode: The Power of Animal Flow 🦎🔥

Ditch the stationary machines of 2025. In 2026, the elite are moving past static lifting and back to primal, ground-based movements. Welcome to Animal Flow.

• Mobility & Strength: You aren't just stretching; you're building explosive power in angles your body has forgotten.
• Core Control: Every millimeter of the movement requires total midsection tension to stay balanced.
• The Result: A lean, naturally athletic physique that moves with lethal grace.

Adho Mukha Gomukhasana ✨

Sometimes, the real magic happens when we slow down and fold inward. This beautiful variation of Cow Face Pose invites a deeper stretch—not just in the hips, but along the back and shoulders too.

As you gently fold over your stacked knees, you may feel intensity… but also release. It’s a reminder that growth often comes with a little discomfort, and with patience, the body opens up in its own time.

Breathe deeply, stay present, and let your body soften into the pose. 🌿

Consistency doesn’t stop when you go on holiday.

Being away doesn’t mean you have to go all in with intense workouts… but it does mean you can keep the body moving. A short gym session, a walk along the beach, a quick mobility routine, it all keeps the engine running.

The truth is, consistency beats perfection every time. When you keep moving, even while you’re away, you come back feeling stronger, sharper, and ready to step straight back into your routine. No starting over. No struggling to find momentum again.

Holidays are for recharging… but that doesn’t mean switching the engine off completely. Keep it ticking over. Your future self will thank you. 💪
