Active Recovery Isn’t Being Lazy 👉 It’s Smart Training

Even on a mini holiday break, I keep my body moving.

Not with intense workouts… but with active recovery.

Active recovery means low intensity movement that helps your body repair, reset and stay mobile while still supporting your fitness, fat loss and strength goals.

For those of us in our 40s and 50s, this is one of the most powerful habits you can build.

Why?

Because active recovery helps:

• Improve muscle recovery
• Reduce stiffness and soreness
• Increase blood circulation and mobility
• Maintain metabolic health
• Keep your training consistency high

When you take a few days away, like I am right now, a little movement, stretching, mobility work or light cardio means you return to full training feeling ready, not starting again from scratch.

That’s the key to long-term fitness and sustainable weight loss.

Train hard.
Recover smart.
Stay consistent.

Your future body will thank you.

I never liked sweet potatoes, especially in savoury meals. I tried to make sweet potato waffles and they completely failed, but the batter itself tasted surprisingly good. Instead of throwing it away, I turned it into a quick breakfast bowl — and it turned out amazing.

The best part is that the sweet potato cooks in about five minutes, so the whole breakfast is very fast to prepare. The hot sweet potato blends with the egg and protein milk to create a creamy base, then oats, nuts and skyr add texture and extra protein.

Per serving: 606 kcal, 38 g protein, 74 g carbs, 19 g fat.

Recipe (1 serving)
185 g sweet potato
1 egg
50 ml protein milk
43 g oats
20 g nuts
150 g white skyr
cinnamon (+ optional stevia)

Cook the sweet potato until soft (about 5 minutes). Blend egg, protein milk and cinnamon. Add the hot sweet potato and blend until smooth. Pour into a bowl, add oats and nuts, and once slightly cooled add skyr on top.

Most people think garlic bread is “just a side.”
But calories add up quickly when fat is involved.
One slice of garlic baguette is usually around 100–130 kcal.
That means 4 slices ≈ 480 kcal.
For the same calories you could eat about 620 g of potatoes.
#calories #nutritionfacts #foodcomparison #fatlossdiet #garlicbread

One can of regular Coke has about 140 kcal. Four cans = ~560 kcal. A typical 100 g milk chocolate bar is also around 530–550 kcal.

The point is not that chocolate is “better.” The point is awareness. Liquid calories are easy to consume, do not create much fullness, and can quietly push your daily intake higher than expected.

If fat loss or weight control is your goal, drinks with sugar can make a bigger difference than most people realize.
#calories #nutritiontip #liquidcalories #fatlosstip  #chocolate

Anavar एक oral anabolic steroid है जो bodybuilding में cutting, fat loss और lean muscle के लिए use किया जाता है।
👉 इसका scientific नाम Oxandrolone है।
👉 ये doctor prescription वाली medicine है — बिना medical guidance के लेना health के लिए खतरनाक हो सकता है।
✅ Anavar क्या है
ये एक synthetic anabolic steroid है
Muscle loss रोकने और recovery के लिए बनाया गया था
Medical use:
Severe burns recovery
Weight loss / muscle wasting diseases
Surgery के बाद recovery
👉 Bodybuilding में इसे cutting steroid कहा जाता है
🔥 Anavar के फायदे (Benefits)
🏋️ Muscle फायदे
Lean muscle gain (पानी नहीं भरता body में)
Muscle hardness & definition बढ़ाता है
Strength बढ़ती है
Cutting में muscle loss रोकता है
🔥 Fat loss फायदे
Metabolism तेज करता है
Body fat कम करने में मदद
Dry & vascular look देता है
💪 Performance फायदे
Recovery fast होती है
Endurance बढ़ती है
Gym performance improve
⭐ Extra benefit
👉 बाकी steroids के मुकाबले mild माना जाता है
❌ Anavar के नुकसान (Side Effects)
❤️ Liver damage
Oral steroid होने की वजह से liver पर stress
Long cycle → liver enzymes बढ़ सकते हैं
💔 Testosterone suppression
Natural testosterone कम हो जाता है
Cycle के बाद weakness, low libido
🧠 Mental side effects
Mood swings
Depression
Aggression
❤️ Heart risk
Bad cholesterol बढ़ता है
Heart disease का risk
🧔 Men में
Hair fall
Acne
Infertility risk
Testicle shrink
👩 Women में (बहुत dangerous)
Voice heavy
Facial hair

👉 Bodybuilding में इसे safe steroid बोलते हैं

HOW TO: PUSH-UPS ADVANCED vs. BEGINNER

Push-ups are one of the most effective bodyweight movements you can do—but only if your form is dialed in.

BEGINNER PUSH-UPS (knees or incline)
DO:
Keep hands stacked under shoulders
Maintain a straight line (shoulders → hips → knees)
Brace your core the entire time
Lower chest toward the ground (not your face)
Keep elbows at ~30–45° from your body
Move slow and controlled
Fully press back up (don’t stop halfway)

DON’T:
Let your hips sag or stick up
Flare elbows straight out to 90°
Drop your head or lead with your chin

ADVANCED PUSH-UPS (standard)
DO:
Form a straight plank (head → heels)
Squeeze glutes + engage core
Keep wrists, elbows, shoulders stacked
Lower until chest is just above the floor
Keep elbows tucked (30–45°)
Control both the lowering AND pushing phase
Keep neck neutral (eyes slightly ahead, not craned)

DON’T:
Arch your lower back
Let hips drop or pike up

HERE’S WHY YOU SHOULD ADD ROWING AS YOUR FINISHER: 

Rowing is a full-body movement that uses about 85% of your muscles in every stroke, making it one of the best ways to build stamina, strength, and overall fitness while strengthening your heart.

Lower body muscles: Quadriceps, Calves & Glutes.

Rowing is also a great alternative to the treadmill or elliptical, especially when you want a powerful finisher after your workout.

A simple but powerful circuit to hit glutes, legs, arms, and core while keeping your heart rate elevated.

10 reps each leg — Targets: Glutes, quads, hamstrings

20 total reps — Targets: Core, obliques
• Lean slightly back
• Rotate through the torso
• Keep chest lifted

I’m 41 — and these are the 5 things I do daily to stay fit, confident, healthy, and grounded.

1. I focus on whole foods and a balanced diet. 🥬🫐
Lean protein, vegetables, fruit, healthy fats, quality carbs. I prioritize nourishment over restriction. Stable blood sugar, proper hydration, and real ingredients keep energy high, inflammation low, and hormones supported.

2. I protect my sleep.🛌😴
Sleep lowers cortisol, regulates hunger hormones, improves recovery, and protects mental clarity. Elevated stress ages the body. Quality rest is essential for fat loss, muscle tone, skin health, and emotional stability.

3. I invest in feeling and looking good.🧖🏻‍♀️💅🏼
Skincare, facials, massages, recovery work — the small details that make me feel polished and refreshed. Confidence impacts posture, discipline, and mindset. Taking care of yourself supports mental health.

4. I move my body every single day.🏃🏻‍♀️💪🏼
Strength training, walking, cardio, mobility. Movement keeps metabolism active, supports heart health, reduces stress, and preserves muscle as we age. Consistency over extremes.

5. I prioritize God and my mental foundation.❤️
Time in the Word. Prayer. Reflection. Protecting my thoughts. A strong spiritual and mental base supports every other habit.

Age isn’t the limit, your standards are.

.

.
🇺🇸Do the movements in order , 74 burpees then, 74 push ups then , 74 sit-ups etc.

.
At the time of his death, KHK Klement and his team were attempting to locate and arrest a number of wanted suspects. During a short foot chase on nearby railroad tracks, KHK Klement was struck by a freight train and killed.

Ai luoja että sattui taas päähän kun kuuntelin perinteistä ruokavalio keskustelua siitä, että hiilihydraatti on pakollinen ravinnon lähde eikä ilman sitä ihminen jaksa tehdä mitään, eikä lihasta voi kasvattaa ilman hiilihydraatin mässäämistä 🤦‍♀️

TÄSSÄ ON asiakkaani Inbody mittaukset, joissa väliä 4kk. Hän syö ketoruokavaliota ja treenaa salilla 2-3krt/viikko perus liikkujan tyylillä eli ei ole super urheilija tai harrasta muuta liikuntaa aktiivisesti. 

Tulokset kertoo, että hän tiputti 4kk:ssa rasvaa 5,2kg!! Samalla hänen lihasmassa kasvoi 900g eli lähes kilon! Tavoite oli rasvanpoltossa ja kiinteytymisessä eli salitreenissä ei ollut edes tavoitteena kasvattaa älyttömästi lihasta, mutta SILTI hän sai lihasmassaa rasvanpolton ollessa hurjaa!! 🔥 💪  Ja otetaan vielä huomioon se, että hänen lähtötilannekin oli normaalipainoinen nainen eikä ylipainoinen ja hän oli liikkunut kuitenkin aiemminkin. 

Keskustelimme pitkät pätkät hänen kanssaan ketoruokavaliosta ja positiivisista vaikutuksista mm. siihen ettei ole mielitekoja herkkuihin, ruoka täyttää ja pitää nälkää hyvin, energiaa on hyvin koko ajan ja hauskaa on sekin, että hän syö rasvaa todella paljon suhteessa normaaliin hyväksyttyyn ja silti viskeraalirasvat tippui hurjasti 🤩 

Voimmeko nyt pikkuhiljaa lopettaa keto ja vh ruokavalion mollaaminen ja ymmärtää, että se on oikeasti tehokas ruokavalio hyvinvoinnille ja treenikehitykselle, kun se tehdään oikein eikä syödä vain pekonia ja munia 😁

Jos sinua kiinnostaa valmennus kanssani, ota yhteyttä dm. Teen niin ruokavalioita kuin treeniohjelmiakin myös etänä.

Hiilaripäivä. Sitä odottaa kuin kuuta nousevaa ja kun se tulee, on se kamalaa. Nyt pari kertaa tehty kuukauden sisään korkeamman hiilarin päiviä ja eri hiilari määrillä, mutta silti olo on ihan paska sen jälkeen. Illalla olen väsynyt kuin kuollut lahna, mikä toki hyvä asia unen kannalta, mutta väsymys seuraa myös seuraavaan päivään. 

Glykogeenit täyttyy, mutta väsymys seuraa treeniin silti eikä treeni tunnu sen tehokkaammalta ainakaan suoralta käsin (ehkä parin päivän päästä on energisempi). Verensokerit pomppaa heti aamusta ylös ilman aamupalaa, kuten joka aterian jälkeenkin ja sitten tulee pistettyä korjaavaa lopulta jopa liikaa. Tekee mieli lyödä itseään, kun pitää taas vetää hiilaria, että korjaa omat virheet ettei kuole 🤦🏻‍♀️

Ja sitten on se NÄLKÄ 🤣 Mikä hemmetti siinä on, että hiilaripäivänä on saatana koko ajan nälkä. Heti ruuan jälkeen on vielä enemmän nälkä kuin ennen ruokaa 😬 Ja ne perkeleen työpaikan kahvipullat oikein huutaa sun nimeä toisin kuin matalan hh päivänä ne pitää turpansa kiinni 🤣 

Jos jotain iloa pitää löytää on se vessareissujen sujuvuus 😆 Ja yleensä painon heilahdus alaspäin, tosin tänään se ei kyllä heilahtanut. Ja kaippa se kroppa siitä sitten rasvaakin polttaa kun pistää hikoilee. Aineenvaihdunta herää vaikka aivot menee sumutilaan 😵‍💫

Löysin koneelta vanhoja kuvia vuoden 2008 kisadietiltä. Tuolloin dietti ei mennyt putkeen...valmentajana oli Aki Mähönen, joka tippui matkasta kesken dietin ja vedin lähes yksin loppuun, mutta pysyin hengissä. Onneksi Lisbeth Hälikkä auttoi sen, mitä pystyi neuvomalla loppurutistukset 💕.Tuon kesäkuun muistan hyvin, koska silloin alkoi kiristyminen toden teolla, kun sain pitää huolta itsestäni oikein. Aika kuitenkin loppui kesken, enkä lopulta ollut niin hyvässä kunnossa kuin olisin halunnut, mutta sentään terveys säilyi 🙏 

Nyt olen kiitollinen valmentajalleni @emmipa_. Olemme hyvässä vauhdissa kohti syksyn kisoja eikä ole tarvinnut kokea ambulanssi kyytejä kertaakaan 🙏 On tärkeää, että terveys on tässä hommassa edellä, vaikka sen rasvankin kuuluu palaa. Diabeetikolla on aina haasteena sokerin lasku ja nousu, eikä silloin mietitä kaloreita, kun pitää estää shokki. Tämä vaatii valmentajalta ymmärrystä ja suunnittelua dietin suhteen paljon enemmän. 

 Kuitenkin tämä on mahdollista myös diabeetikolle 💪♥️

Fat loss isn’t magic… it’s MATH! 🚨
1kg of body fat = ~7700 kcal of stored energy 💪
Want to lose 1kg? Create a 7700 kcal deficit (diet + exercise).
Want to gain 1kg? Eat 7700 kcal ABOVE maintenance over time.
Real talk examples:
🔥 +500 kcal surplus/day → ~1kg fat gain in just ~15 days
🔥 -500 kcal deficit/day → ~1kg fat loss in ~15 days
But go too fast and you risk losing muscle. Smart move? Aim for 0.5–1kg/week with high protein + strength training to keep that metabolism firing 🔥
Master this rule and stop guessing—start winning your body composition game!

Honey in your chai or that diet cola fizz? Which one’s actually better for you? 🤔
Natural sweeteners (honey, stevia, maple syrup) come straight from nature – minimally processed, with some antioxidants, vitamins, and that real flavor kick. They have calories, but they feel more ‘whole’ and can support gut health in moderation.
Artificial ones (aspartame, sucralose, saccharin) are lab-made for zero/low calories – great for cutting sugar without spiking blood sugar, but recent research raises flags on gut microbiome disruption, potential metabolic effects, and long-term use links to cravings or other issues.
Bottom line: Neither is ‘evil’ – it’s about balance! Swap processed sugars for natural where you can (drizzle honey on yogurt or use stevia in desserts), but if you’re managing weight/diabetes, artificial can be a tool in limited amounts.

Time to retire these protein myths for good! 💪🥦
Myth 1: ‘High protein destroys your kidneys’ → Nope! Science says for healthy people (no pre-existing kidney issues), even higher protein is safe & doesn’t cause damage. Your kidneys adapt — no harm done.
Myth 2: ‘You only need protein if you’re lifting heavy’ → Wrong! Everyone needs protein daily for muscles, immunity, hair, skin, hormones — not just gym rats.
Myth 3: ‘Plant-based can’t build muscle or meet needs’ → Totally can! Daal, chickpeas, paneer, soy, nuts, quinoa — combine them smartly and you’re golden. No need for whey 24/7.
Fact: Aim for 0.8-1g/kg body weight minimum (more like 1.2-2g/kg if active/training). Your body thrives on it — no matter your routine!

March is Colorectal Cancer Awareness Month – and it’s hitting closer to home than ever.
This silent disease is now the #1 cancer killer in people under 50… but the truth? Most cases are preventable. Early detection saves lives – survival jumps to 90%+ when caught soon!
Watch for these red flags (don’t ignore them!):
🔴 Blood in stool
🤢 Persistent abdominal pain or bloating
⚖️ Unexplained weight loss
😴 Constant fatigue
🚽 Changes in bowel habits (diarrhea/constipation)
Reduce your risk today – small changes add up:
🥦 Load up on fruits, veggies & fiber (think desi sabzi, salads, fruits)
🏃‍♀️ Stay active – even daily walks count
🚭 Say no to tobacco
⚖️ Keep a healthy weight
🍷 Limit alcohol
Screening starts at 45 (earlier if family history or symptoms). Talk to your doctor – early action = more time with loved ones.

Recipe 

1. The “Crunch” Prep
Heat a heavy-bottomed pan with 1 tsp ghee (or dry roast). Add the Makhana and roast on low flame for 5–7 minutes until they snap easily between your fingers.

Divide the roasted Makhana into two parts:
• Part A (70%): Pulse in a blender or crush with a rolling pin into a coarse powder. This acts as a natural thickener so you don’t need cornstarch or condensed milk.

• Part B (30%): Keep them whole for that classic “bite.

3. The Simmer
Bring the milk to a boil in a deep pan. Add the saffron milk and cardamom powder. Reduce the heat and let it simmer for 5 minutes.

4. Combining
Add both the crushed and whole Makhana to the milk. Stir continuously. The crushed Makhana will absorb the milk and thicken the consistency within 10–12 minutes on low heat.

5. The Finishing Touch
Switch off the flame. Let it cool for 2 minutes (to prevent curdling) and then stir in the jaggery powder or stevia. Garnish with slivered nuts.

• Serve Chilled: It thickens further as it cools.

Trying to lose weight but the scale isn’t moving?
You might be making these common nutrition mistakes — even if you’re eating “healthy”.
Here are 5 mistakes that slow fat loss:
1️⃣ Skipping Protein
Protein keeps you full, supports muscle, and helps burn more calories.
2️⃣ Drinking Your Calories
Sugary drinks, juices, and fancy coffees add hidden calories.
3️⃣ Eating “Healthy” But High-Calorie Foods
Foods like granola, nuts, and smoothies can add up quickly.
4️⃣ Not Eating Enough Fiber
Fiber helps control hunger and stabilizes blood sugar.
5️⃣ Poor Meal Timing
Long gaps between meals can increase cravings and overeating.
💡 Quick Tip:
Add protein + fiber to every meal to stay full longer.

You don’t grow in the gym from just showing up…

You grow from how HARD you push when you’re there.

Anyone can do 10 reps.

But not everyone can do 10 reps
when it burns…
when it hurts…
when your mind says stop.

That’s where the body changes.

Intensity =
more muscle stimulus
more calories burned
more progress in less time.

Low intensity workouts?

That’s just maintenance.

If you want a different body…
you need a different level of effort.

“If you’re tired of training and seeing no results…
DM me ‘RELENTLESS ’ and I’ll show you how to train properly.”

You keep changing your workout split…
but your body stays the same.

Push. Pull. Legs.
Upper. Lower.
Bro split.

None of that is your problem.

Your problem is execution.

You don’t push hard enough.
You don’t track progress.
You repeat the same weights… every week.

And then wonder why nothing changes.

Muscle doesn’t care about your plan.
It responds to stress.

No progression = no reason to grow.

Be honest…
are your lifts actually improving?

#Hand Position & Grip: Place hands shoulder-width apart, fingers spread wide, and actively grip the floor with your fingertips to control balance, as this acts as your “brakes”.
Alignment: Stack your shoulders over your wrists and maintain a tight, hollow-body position (slightly tucked pelvis) to avoid excessive arching.
Strength Development: Key exercises include wall-assisted handstands (chest-to-wall), handstand push-ups, and holding a “pike” position.
Overcoming Fear: Practice falling, specifically the “wheel out” (falling forward) to learn how to bail safely.
Progression: Start by walking your feet up a wall, slowly getting closer to the wall until your body is fully vertical, eventually attempting freestanding holds.

This account started because real life is messy.

Some days you’re showing up as your best self.

Some days you’re just showing up.

And sometimes… that’s actually good enough.

Life moves through stages. The kind where you’re building habits. The kind where everything feels like it’s working. And the kind where it feels like you accidentally landed in the upside down and you’re just trying to keep things moving forward.

There are moments where you learn to say “beat it” to things that don’t belong in your life anymore.

Moments where you realize it’s actually okay to want to be friends with your kid’s friend’s mom.

Moments where you look around and think…

How did life get this weird… and this beautiful… at the same time?

And even on the days that feel chaotic, exhausting, or a little upside down…

You’d still do the day the same way again.

Because you showed up.

You left a footprint somewhere.

Maybe in your own life.
Maybe in your kid’s.
Maybe in your partner’s.
Maybe in a friend’s.
Maybe in the life of someone you don’t even realize noticed.

That’s what this space is about.

Not perfection.
Not hustle culture.
Not becoming some ideal version of yourself.

But finding the habits that work for the moment you’re in.

Because habits still matter.

It’s what you choose.
And what you decide not to choose.

Those choices shape you.

They shape the life you’re building.

So if you’re here…
maybe it’s because you’re ready to grow too.

Welcome to real.life.habits.

Sometimes your brain just needs a reset.

Not a full routine.
Not an hour of meditation.
Just 30 seconds to refocus.

The 3x3 method.

It’s commonly used to reduce anxiety, but I also use it when my mind is scattered and I need to come back to the present moment.

Here’s how it works:

1️⃣ Slow your movement and name 3 things you see.
2️⃣ Close your eyes and name 3 things you hear.
3️⃣ Move 3 different parts of your body, one at a time.

That’s it.

In less than a minute you bring your brain back to the present instead of letting it run ahead into stress, worry, or overwhelm.

Small resets like this are what real life habits are made of.

Nothing complicated.
Just simple tools you can use anywhere, anytime.

In 2016, Japanese biologist Yoshinori Ohsumi won the Nobel Prize for proving something ancient traditions knew all along…

When your body stops receiving food..
It doesn’t shut down…
It cleans house.

Through a process called autophagy, your cells begin to:
✅ Consume damaged proteins

✅ Slow the aging process

And it only activates when you stop eating.

Short fasts.
Empty stomach walks.
Early dinners.

Not a diet, a biological reset.

Drinking a glass of water before sleeping may support heart health by preventing overnight dehydration. When you’re dehydrated, blood can become slightly thicker, which may increase strain on the heart and blood vessels during sleep.

What research suggests:
Studies published in cardiovascular and hydration research journals show that proper hydration helps maintain healthy blood circulation, supports blood pressure balance, and reduces stress on the heart—important factors linked to lower risk of heart attack and stroke.

Why nighttime hydration matters:
	•	Your body loses water while sleeping (breathing & sweating)
	•	Mild dehydration can increase blood viscosity
	•	Proper hydration supports smoother blood flow to the heart and brain

How to do it right:
	•	Drink 1 small glass (150–200 ml) of plain water
	•	Avoid excess water if you have frequent nighttime urination
	•	Stop drinking at least 30 minutes before sleep

Important note (Disclaimer):
This is a general wellness tip, not a medical treatment. Drinking water alone cannot prevent heart attack or stroke. If you have heart disease, kidney issues, or fluid restrictions, consult your doctor.

Researchers from Harvard University followed hundreds of families for more than 85 years and uncovered an interesting insight: having a sister can influence a person’s life more deeply than many other family relationships — and the impact goes beyond simple affection.

1. Lower stress levels
People who have sisters tend to show 15–20% lower levels of cortisol, the body’s main stress hormone. Even if they live far apart and rarely meet, the sense of having someone who shares your childhood memories — from embarrassing moments to family traditions — can reduce anxiety. Your brain recognizes there’s someone with whom you can be completely yourself.

2. A sister as emotional support
During stressful or overwhelming times, a sister often acts like a natural emotional regulator. Sometimes a short conversation is enough to ease tension. She may not solve the issue directly, but her presence makes the situation feel less overwhelming. Simply listening and reminding you that you’re not alone can help calm the mind.

3. Support during major life changes
Women with sisters are statistically less likely to experience depression during difficult transitions such as divorce or menopause. Having someone who shares your history — who can laugh about old memories or childhood stories — helps people recover emotionally from challenging periods.

4. Emotional development in childhood
Children raised with sisters often develop stronger emotional awareness. By around age 10, they may be better at recognizing feelings in others, forming social connections, and handling conflicts like bullying. Food is emotional. And most people don’t realize it.

I’m a huge stress eater. When I get stressed out, I just munch. Last year during my cut, I would go through a container of mixed nuts and raisins like it was nothing every week.

Healthy? Sure. But a handful of nuts is a couple hundred calories. Imagine just mindlessly eating those.

That’s why I like fasting at the beginning of the year. It helps you dissociate your emotional relationship with food.

When you fast, you start to see how much of your eating is actually hunger vs. habit, stress, boredom, or comfort.

→ Recognize when you’re stress eating
→ Use fasting as a reset tool
→ Electrolytes make a world of difference
→ Break the emotional connection

Your relationship with food matters more than the food itself.

Stop overcomplicating your diet.

Here’s how I keep nutrition stupid simple:

BULKING:
→ Protein at every meal (eggs, chicken, steak)
→ Add rice and potatoes for extra carbs
→ Whole foods. Things you need to cook.
→ Just eat. A lot.

CUTTING:
→ Same protein, drop the carbs
→ Slow carbs only: broccoli, asparagus, green beans, brussels sprouts
→ Avocado & mixed nuts for healthy fats

The biggest unlock? Track your macros for one year. Every single thing you eat.

After that? You’ll be able to look at any plate and know exactly what’s in it. 20g protein here, 40g there. You’ll never need an app again.

Save this. Screenshot it. Use it.

Here’s something most people get wrong in the gym:

They do flat bench, incline bench, decline bench, flies — and by the time they get to that last exercise, they’re gassed. It’s probably not even doing anything.

The truth? You’re getting 90% of your results from that FIRST exercise of the day.

So stop saving energy for the back end. Go ALL OUT on that first lift. Go to failure. Leave nothing in the tank.

The rest of your workout? It’s accessories and sprinkles.

→ Pick 2 main movements
→ Go to failure on both
→ Everything else is supplemental

Keep it simple, stupid! (KISS)

Here's what most people misunderstand about hard things: it's not the accomplishment that changes you. It's not the medal. Not the title. Not the number on the scale or the promotion.

So push yourself. Mentally. Physically. Emotionally. Creatively. Not because suffering is noble, but because growth lives on the other side of discomfort.

And in time, you will have arrived. Not at a destination—but at a version of yourself you once thought was out of reach.

The ability to lean into hard things is a muscle. Like any muscle, it atrophies when you don't use it. And like any muscle, you can grow it—deliberately, consistently, through resistance.

Do something difficult every day. Not when you feel like it. Not when the stars align. Every single day.

→ Exercise before your brain has time to negotiate
→ Sit in the sauna until you want to quit—then stay longer
→ Take the cold plunge and hold your ground
→ Read when you'd rather scroll
→ Write when you have nothing to say

None of these feel good in the moment. The immediate payoff is invisible. But when you zoom out—weeks, months, years—these small acts of daily resistance compound into something unrecognizable.

The person you kept waiting to become? You're building them right now, one hard choice at a time.

Save this list. Start tomorrow morning.

#discipline #dailypractice #compoundeffect #coldplunge #sauna #grind #fitness #unrelentingpressure

Hard things build you. They forge character, demand effort, and resist every shortcut you try to take. That's not a bug—it's the feature.

But most people spend their entire lives running from difficulty. They optimize for comfort. They negotiate with the voice that says "do it tomorrow." And they wonder why nothing changes.

Here's the uncomfortable truth you already know but keep avoiding: everything you want is on the other side of the work you've been putting off.

→ The career you want? Behind the hard conversations you're avoiding.
→ The body you want? Behind the early mornings and the discipline.
→ The life you want? Behind the fear you keep calling "practicality."

Nobody ever became extraordinary by staying comfortable.

Stop running. Start building. Full post on the blog. Link in bio.

#hardwork #discipline #mindset #grind #noexcuses #selfimprovement #unrelentingpressure

If you can wake your ass up super early and crush your workout first thing, you will outcompete every single person around you.

I don’t care how talented someone is. If they’re sleeping in while you’re grinding at 6am, you’ve already won.

→ Wake up earlier
→ Get to the gym first thing
→ Build that routine
→ The rest takes care of itself

This is the one habit that changes everything. Not a supplement. Not a program. Just winning the morning.

很多人以為自己很累，
是因為事情太多。

其實不是。

是因為內耗太多。

事情還沒發生，
你已經在腦袋裡
失敗過好幾次。

怕做不好
怕被評價
怕走錯

於是一直想，
卻沒有開始。

你有沒有發現？

有些人不是比較厲害，
只是沒有想那麼多。

而你，
在還沒出發之前，
就已經把自己困住了。

最累的不是外在的壓力，
是你心裡那個
一直否定你的聲音。

它不大聲，
但每天都在。

慢慢地，
你開始不敢嘗試，
不敢相信自己，
甚至連想要改變
都變得很累。

什麼都還沒發生，
你卻已經先累了。

很多時候
不是事情有多難，

而是停在原地
反覆想、反覆糾結，
更消耗。

有時候不一定會變簡單，

但至少，
會比一直卡在那裡想，
來得輕鬆一點。

慢慢來也沒關係。

先動一點點，
就很好了💗

Have you ever felt tired
before anything even happened?

Not from doing too much—
but from thinking too much.

You’ve already failed in your head
before you even begin.

Sometimes it’s not the situation
that drains you—
it’s staying stuck in it.

It may not be easier,
but moving—even a little—
feels lighter than staying in your head.

The Viral Street Corn Taco Bowls 🌽✨

This is such an easy, high-protein meal that we will be making again and again!!

What you need:
• 2 sweet potatoes
• 2 lbs ground chicken (or ground meat of choice)
• Avocado oil spray
• Salt, pepper & garlic powder
• 1 packet mild Siete taco seasoning

Street Corn Mixture:
• 1 cup cottage cheese
• 1 tbsp mayonnaise
• 1 can corn, rinsed & drained
• 1 avocado, diced
• Juice of ½ lime
• 2 jalapeños, finely diced
• 1 bunch cilantro, chopped
• Tajín, to taste

Instructions:
	1.	Peel and chop the sweet potatoes into bite-sized pieces. Spray with avocado oil and toss with salt, pepper, and garlic powder. Air fry at 400°F for ~15 minutes until tender and slightly crispy.
	2.	Heat avocado oil in a pan over medium heat and brown the ground chicken until fully cooked. Stir in the mild taco seasoning.
	3.	In a blender or food processor, blend the cottage cheese + mayonnaise until smooth.
	4.	In a large bowl combine the corn, diced avocado, lime juice, jalapeños, cilantro, cottage cheese mixture, and Tajín. Mix well.
	5.	Assemble bowls with sweet potatoes on the bottom (add rice if you want it extra filling), top with the taco meat and a generous scoop of the street corn mixture. Finish with extra Tajín and a drizzle of hot honey.

Serve with tortilla chips and it's sooo good 🤍

#easymeals #highproteinrecipes #tacobowls #streetcorn #easydinner

Easy Chicken Tikka Masala 🍛✨🔥

What you need:
- 1.5 lbs chicken breast, cut into bite sized pieces 
- 2 packets of Trader Joe's Tikka Masala Curry Sauce 
- 1/4 cup of plain Greek yogurt 
- salt, pepper 
- 2-4 tablespoons of heavy cream or full fat coconut milk/cream
- sides: zucchini (or veg of choice), garlic naan (from tjs), white rice 

Instructions:
1. Cut chicken into small bite sized pieces and place into a large zip lock bag. Mix in 1/4 cup of Greek yogurt and salt/pepper. Marinate in fridge for at least 30 minutes (up to 2-4 hours). This will help tenderize the chicken. 
2. Dump zip lock bag in a pan over medium heat and cook chicken all the way through. Can pour out some excess juice if needed. 
3. Add in both tikka masala curry packets and let simmer for 3-5 minutes until heated through. Add in heavy cream or coconut milk and simmer for another 1-2 minutes.
4. Serve over white rice with veg of choice and naan. ✨

Roasted Tomato Soup & Grilled Cheese on a Snow Day ❄️ = Nothing Better ✨

Roasted Tomato Soup 🍅
Ingredients:
• 10–12 Roma tomatoes
• 2 small (or 1 large) yellow onion
• 1–2 red peppers
• 2 garlic bulbs
• Olive oil
• 2 cups chicken broth (or veggie broth)
• Drizzle of balsamic vinegar
• 2–3 tbsp tomato paste
• ½ cup heavy cream
• Salt, pepper, red pepper flakes, Italian seasoning
• Fresh basil
• A couple pinches of sugar

Instructions:
1️⃣ Chop tomatoes, onion, and red pepper into quarters. Slice the tops off the garlic bulbs. Add everything to a lined baking sheet, drizzle with balsamic, season with salt/pep, red pepper flakes, Italian seasoning, and finish with lots of olive oil.
2️⃣ Roast at 400°F for 25-30 minutes until soft and slightly charred.
3️⃣ Let cool for 10-15 minutes, then add roasted veggies and squeezed garlic to a blender. Add basil, tomato paste, sugar, and broth.
4️⃣ Pour into a large pot and simmer for 10–15 minutes.
5️⃣ Stir in a couple dollops of butter and the heavy cream.
6️⃣ Season to taste with more salt & pepper if needed. 
7️⃣ Serve warm with an extra drizzle of cream and basil (dried or fresh) on top.

Grilled Cheese Tips 🧀 
• Freshly grated cheese melts best (I love mixing different cheeses but always use Unexpected Cheddar from TJ’s)
• Sprinkle everything bagel seasoning inside with the cheese

Kung Pao Chicken Stir-Fry with Broccoli ✨ 

Ingredients:
- 1-1.5 chicken breast
- 1 red bell pepper (chopped)
- yellow onion (chopped) or can use green onions 
- broccoli (smaller pieces)
- cornstarch
- sesame oil 
- roasted peanuts 
- salt/pepper/garlic powder
- kung pao sauce ingredients (see below)
- white rice
- sesame seeds to garnish 
- Siracha/ chili crunch (optional)

Kung Pao Sauce:
- 1/2 cup of coconut aminos
- 2 tablespoons of honey
- 2 tablespoons of rice wine vinegar 
- 1 teaspoon fish sauce (or can omit)
- 2 teaspoons sesame oil
- 2-3 cloves of garlic
- Siracha and chili crunch (your preference - I add a lot)
- 1 1/2 teaspoon of corn starch (whisk in completely)
- 1/3 cup of cold water to thin out sauce 

Instructions:
1. Wash chicken breasts & dry completely. Chop chicken breast into bite sized pieces and put in a zip lock bag. Add in 1 tablespoon of cornstarch, salt, pepper, garlic powder. Shake bag to coat chicken fully.
2. Add sesame oil to a pan over medium heat and cook chicken until cooked through then set aside.
3. Start making sauce while chicken cooks and finish while vegs cook. 
4. In same pan as chicken, add more sesame oil and then add vegs - onion, red pepper, broccoli. Cook vegs until soft - season with salt/pepper 
5. Add the chicken back in with the vegs then pour in the sauce. Add in the peanuts. Cook for a few minutes until sauce thickens. 
6. Serve over white rice, top with more chili crunch/Siracha and sesame seeds 🫶🏼

#easydinner #healthymeals #chickendinner #kungpaochicken #food

Garlic Shrimp Linguine ✨

This was soooo good. I'm surprised I could wait long enough to take the photo. 

What you need:
- 1 lb shrimp
- pasta of choice 
- 3 cloves of garlic 
- 1/2 cup heavy whipping cream
- 2 tablespoons of butter 
- cherry tomatoes halved 
- handful of spinach
- reserved pasta water (1/2 - 1 cup)
- parm 
- parsley (garnish)

Instructions
1. Defrost shrimp, dry completely, put in zip lock bag to toss with dash of olive oil, salt, pepper, paprika, red pepper flakes
2. Bring a pot of water to a boil, salt generously, and cook pasta until al dente. Reserve 1 cup of pasta water, then drain.
3. Heat a pan over medium heat. Once hot, add olive oil and the seasoned shrimp. Cook until done, then set aside.
4. In the same pan, turn heat to low and add the butter. Add the minced garlic and cook for about 30 seconds, just until fragrant. (Be sure to bring to low to avoid burning garlic/butter)
5. (Optional) Add a splash of white wine and let it cook down for 30–60 seconds.
Pour in the heavy whipping cream and stir for 2–3 minutes, scraping up any browned bits. Add reserved pasta water a little at a time until the sauce reaches a silky consistency.
6. Add the halved cherry tomatoes and let them soften for 1–2 minutes. Toss in the spinach and stir until coated and wilted. Bring the sauce to a simmer.
7. Add the shrimp and cooked pasta into the pan. Toss everything together, adding a little more pasta water if needed. Stir until everything is coated and shrimp is warmed. 
8. Finish by squeezing a little bit of lemon over top. Top with freshly grated parm and fresh parsley. 🫶🏼

#dinner #shrimp #pasta #easymeal #shrimppasta #easydinner #yummy #chef #cooking

The Best Blueberry Muffins ✨🫐

Muffin Ingredients (makes 24 muffins)
- 3 cups of flour
- 1 1/2 cup of granulated sugar 
- 1 tablespoon, 1 teaspoon of baking powder 
- 1 teaspoon of salt
- 2/3 cup of vegetable oil
- 2 large eggs (room temp if possible)
- 1/2 cup of milk (more as needed)
- 2 1/2 cup of blueberries 
- Lemon zest from one lemon
- 2 teaspoons of vanilla 

Crumble topping
- 1/2 cup melted butter
- 1/2 cup of flour
- 1/3 cup of granulated sugar
- 3 tablespoons of brown sugar 
- 1 tablespoon of cinnamon 

Instructions:
1. Preheat oven to 400 degrees
2. Line 24 muffin tins with liners
3. Mix together all dry ingredients (flour, granulated sugar, baking powder, salt)
4. Mix together all wet ingredients (vegetable oil, eggs, milk, vanilla)
5. Combine wet ingredients into dry ingredients (add lemon zest) and mix all until combined but don't over mix 
6. Add in more milk as needed until you get the right consistency 
7. Fold in blueberries 
8. Use cookie scooper to scoop into lined muffin tins (filling up to rim) 
9. Make crumble topping by combining melted butter, flour, sugars, and cinnamon 
10. Add a generous amount of the crumble topping to each muffin (can top with more brown sugar and cinnamon if you want!)
11. Bake on 400 for 20-25 minutes until a toothpick comes out clean!
12. Let cool 5-10 minutes before eating!

#muffins #blueberrymuffins #baking #baker #dessert #easyrecipe #cooking #breakfast

Not sure if your muscles are actually growing? Your body is already giving you the signs. 💪📈

If your strength is increasing, muscles look fuller, and your clothes feel tighter, you’re making real progress. Faster recovery, better mind-muscle connection, and even increased appetite are all signs your body is adapting and building muscle.

Growth isn’t always instant in the mirror — but it shows up in your performance, recovery, and daily strength.

Stay consistent, trust the process, and keep pushing. Your gains are happening. 🔥

Hashtags:
#MuscleGrowth #GymProgress #StayConsistent 💪

Want to build muscle faster? It starts with what’s on your plate. 💪🍗

Training is only half the equation — nutrition is what fuels muscle growth. Foods like chicken, eggs, salmon, and Greek yogurt provide high-quality protein your muscles need to repair and grow. Carbs like rice and oats give you the energy to train harder, while healthy fats from nuts support overall recovery.

If you’re not eating enough of the right foods, your progress will always be limited — no matter how hard you train.

Build your meals around protein, carbs, and healthy fats, and your results will follow.

Eat right. Train hard. Grow stronger. 🔥

Hashtags:
#MuscleBuilding #FitnessNutrition #EatForGains 💪

Building muscle but not seeing results? You might be making these common mistakes. 🚫💪

Muscle growth isn’t just about lifting weights — it’s about doing the right things consistently. Not eating enough protein or calories, skipping rest days, poor sleep, and lifting without proper intensity can all slow your progress.

Many people train hard but ignore recovery, nutrition, and progressive overload — and that’s where results get blocked.

The good news? Once you fix these mistakes, your body responds quickly.
Train smart, eat right, recover well — and watch your progress accelerate. 🔥

Hashtags:
#MuscleBuilding #GymMistakes #FitnessTips 💪

Excessive Hairfall Issues 🔥🔥

#hairfallproblem #hairfallsolution #hairfalltreatment

Your progress doesn’t happen during the workout — it happens during recovery. 🔁💪

If you want to get stronger, build muscle, and avoid injuries, you need to recover the right way. Hydration, proper nutrition (protein + carbs), quality sleep, and rest days are essential for repairing muscle fibers and restoring energy.

Ignoring recovery can lead to fatigue, soreness, and slower progress, no matter how hard you train.

The smartest athletes don’t just train hard — they recover smart.

Take care of your body, and it will perform better every time you step into the gym. 🔥

Hashtags:
#WorkoutRecovery #TrainSmart #MuscleRecovery 💪

Strength isn’t built overnight — but your body shows signs when it’s happening. 💪📈

If you’re lifting heavier, doing more reps, recovering faster, and feeling more energetic, you’re on the right track. Even improvements in form, control, and consistency are powerful indicators that your strength is increasing.

Progress isn’t always obvious in the mirror, but it shows up in your performance. Every extra rep, every added weight, every better workout — it all counts.

Stay consistent, trust the process, and keep pushing. Your strength is growing every single day. 🔥

Hashtags:
#StrengthTraining #Progress #FitnessJourney 💪

Struggling to lose fat even after putting in the effort? The problem might be your daily habits. 🚫🔥

Fat loss isn’t just about workouts — it’s about what you do consistently every day. Habits like skipping meals, poor sleep, low protein intake, high sugar consumption, and chronic stress can silently block your progress.

Even if you’re training hard, these factors can disrupt your hormones, increase cravings, and slow your metabolism — making fat loss much harder than it should be.

The key isn’t doing more — it’s fixing what’s holding you back. Once you correct these habits, your results become faster, easier, and more sustainable.

Fix your habits. Unlock real fat loss. 💪🔥

Hashtags:
#FatLossTips #FitnessMistakes #HealthyHabits 💪

Overthinking people must watch this 🌟🔥

#overthinking #stressrelief #healthylifestyle

Gaining weight but not sure why? It’s not random — there’s always a reason. ⚠️📈

Weight gain usually comes down to a few key factors: eating more calories than your body needs, poor sleep, high stress, low activity, and hidden calories from sugar or drinks. 

These small habits add up over time and slowly push your body into a calorie surplus.

The mistake most people make is focusing only on diet, while ignoring sleep, stress, and daily movement — all of which play a huge role in weight management.

The solution isn’t extreme dieting. It’s identifying the real cause and fixing your daily habits.

Once you fix the root problem, results become much easier and sustainable. 💪🔥

Hashtags:
#WeightGain #FatLossTips #HealthyHabits 💪

Fat loss isn’t always about the number on the scale — your body gives you clear signs of progress. 🔥💪

If you’re feeling more energetic, stronger in workouts, sleeping better, and your clothes fit looser, these are powerful indicators that your body is burning fat. 

Even reduced cravings and improved mood show that your metabolism and hormones are working in your favor.

Remember, real fat loss is slow, steady, and sustainable. Don’t rely only on the scale — pay attention to how your body feels and performs.

Stay consistent, trust the process, and keep going. Your results are already happening. 🔥

Hashtags:
#FatLossProgress #StayConsistent #FitnessJourney 💪

What you do at night can either support your fat loss… or slow it down. 🌙🔥

Evening habits play a huge role in your results. Eating a light, balanced dinner, avoiding late-night snacking, and taking a short walk can help your body stay in a calorie deficit. 

At the same time, reducing screen time, managing stress, and getting quality sleep keeps your hormones in check and improves recovery.

Most people focus only on workouts — but real transformation happens through daily habits, especially at night.

If you want better results, start winning your evenings. Small changes before bed can lead to big fat loss over time.

Control your nights. Transform your body. 💪🔥

#FatLossTips #HealthyHabits #NightRoutine 💪

Struggling to stay in a calorie deficit? The secret isn’t eating less — it’s eating smarter. 🔥🥗

Choosing the right foods can help you feel full, satisfied, and energized while still keeping your calories in check. Foods like lean protein, fiber-rich fruits, vegetables, and low-calorie high-volume meals help control hunger and prevent overeating.

The goal isn’t to starve — it’s to stay full while consuming fewer calories. That’s how sustainable fat loss actually works.

When you build your meals around these foods, staying in a calorie deficit becomes easier, more enjoyable, and long-lasting.

Eat more of the right foods, not less of everything. 💪🔥

Hashtags:
#CalorieDeficit #FatLossTips #HealthyEating 💪

Transform your morning routine & habits with these 7 steps:

1. Set a “reverse alarm”
Instead of waking up to noise, wake up to light. I use the hatch and set it to turn on the light 30 min before the actual alarm. There’s also cheaper options on Amazon if you don’t want to buy the hatch.

2. Keep water right next to your bed
When you wake up, immediately drink water to get your body hydrated.

3. 5 second rule by @melrobbins 
Wake up and give yourself a 5 second countdown before you jump out of bed. It actually works so well if you stick to it.

4. Cold water or ice roll your face
This will help you wake up and spike alertness instantly.

5. Put your phone across the room
If you have your phone right next to your bed you probably will be tempted to scroll when you wake up. Having it across the room will help you get up quicker.

6. Prep your gym clothes the night before
If you have your clothes laid out it will take less time to get ready and will be way easier to get ready to workout in the morning.

7. Brush your teeth right when you get up
This will also help you get up and wake up.

That’s the myth.

The truth?
It’s the pressure on the ball of your foot.

Things like:
	•	tight calves
	•	stiff toes
	•	weak foot strength

all shift more load forward…
so the pain keeps coming back—even in “good” shoes.

This is why quick fixes don’t work.

👉 Real fixes come from understanding what’s actually causing it.

⸻

The Shoulder is the Most Complex Joint in the Body 🤔

It’s a FACT—and that’s exactly why your training matters.

Your shoulders are built for mobility, not stability… which means how you train them can make or break your progress.

🏋️‍♀️ Want stronger, healthier shoulders?
✔️ Prioritize warm-ups
✔️ Train stability (not just strength)
✔️ Focus on form
✔️ Balance push + pull movements

Strong shoulders aren’t just about lifting heavier—they’re about moving better, staying pain-free, and building longevity.

Don’t just train hard… train smart.

#MythOrFact #ShoulderHealth #TrainSmart #FitnessTips #InjuryPrevention

High heart rate = working hard.

Your body uses different fuels at different intensities. Fat loss comes down to what you do consistently over time—not just one hard workout.

✔️ Strength + movement

Train smarter, not just harder.

your body might be asking for recovery, not more workouts. #RecoveryMatters #Mobility #StrengthTraining  #Wellness #ActiveAging

Saunas can spike hormones… but that doesn’t mean they build muscle.

You’ve probably seen posts claiming sauna use boosts growth hormone 500%+ and therefore helps build muscle or burn fat.

But here’s the key detail those posts leave out:

Short-term hormone spikes don’t equal real training adaptations.

The best available evidence shows that passive heat exposure (like sauna use) does not consistently improve metabolic outcomes such as muscle growth or fat loss.

What does build muscle?

• Progressive resistance training
• Adequate protein
• Recovery and sleep
• Consistency over time

Saunas can still be great for:

• Relaxation
• Stress reduction
• Circulation
• Recovery

Just don’t confuse temporary hormone changes with actual body composition results.

Evidence matters more than hype.

🍪 Healthy Breakfast Cookies

Who says cookies can’t be breakfast? These are soft, naturally sweetened, and loaded with oats, seeds, and chocolate chips. Perfect for busy mornings, meal prep, or a quick snack when you need something satisfying.

✨ Makes 16 cookies

Ingredients
• 3 medium ripe bananas, mashed
• ½ cup natural peanut butter
• ¼ cup pure maple syrup
• 1 egg
• 2 tsp vanilla extract
• 2 cups Kodiak protein oats
• 3 tbsp flaxseed meal
• ½ tsp baking soda
• ½ tsp kosher salt
• ½ tsp cinnamon
• 1 tbsp chia seeds
• ⅓ cup chocolate chips
• Optional add-ons — sunflower seeds, dried cranberries, chopped pecans, shredded coconut

Instructions
	1.	Preheat oven to 350°F and line a baking sheet with parchment.
	2.	In a bowl, mix bananas, peanut butter, maple syrup, egg, and vanilla.
	3.	Stir in oats, flaxseed, baking soda, salt, cinnamon, and chia seeds.
	4.	Fold in chocolate chips, sunflower seeds, cranberries, and pecans.
	5.	Scoop onto baking sheet and slightly flatten.
	6.	Bake 14–16 minutes until set and lightly golden.

Let cool and enjoy! These store great for quick breakfasts all week.

#healthybreakfast #highproteinrecipes #mealprepideas #healthycookies #fitnessfood proteinrecipes healthysnacks

✨ High Protein Banana Oat Muffins ✨

Soft, fluffy, naturally sweet, and packed with protein — these banana oat muffins are perfect for breakfast, a snack, or meal prep for the week. They’re made with simple ingredients and come together quickly. I love having these on hand when I want something sweet that still helps hit my protein goals. 💪🍌

Macros (per muffin):🔥 110 calories💪 9g protein🍞 16g carbs🥜 1g fat

Recipe (makes 12 muffins)

Ingredients• ¾ cup Kodiak protein oats• ½ cup all-purpose flour• ¾ cup protein powder• 1 tsp baking soda• 1½ tsp baking powder• 1 tsp kosher salt• 1 tsp cinnamon• 1 large egg• 3 ripe bananas, mashed (about 1¼ cup)• ½ cup plain Greek yogurt• 2 tbsp maple syrup (or honey)• 2 tbsp unsweetened applesauce• 1 tsp vanilla extract 
Optional: chocolate chips or pecans/walnuts

Instructions

Preheat oven to 350°F and lightly grease or line a muffin tin.

In a large bowl, mix oats, flour, protein powder, baking soda, baking powder, salt, and cinnamon.

In another bowl, whisk together mashed bananas, egg, Greek yogurt, maple syrup, applesauce, and vanilla.

Add the wet ingredients to the dry ingredients and mix until just combined.

Divide batter evenly into 12 muffin cups.

Bake 15-16 minutes or until a toothpick comes out clean.

Let cool slightly and enjoy! 🙌

Healthy Oatmeal Peanut Butter Cookies 🍪🥜

Soft, naturally sweetened, and made with simple ingredients. These cookies are perfect for a quick breakfast, snack, or healthy dessert. They’re flourless, easy to make, and great for meal prep!
(Special thanks to Kingston for the video!)

✨ Macros (per cookie – makes 12):
🍪 141 calories
💪 4g protein
🍌 19g carbs
🥜 6g fat

Ingredients

• 1 cup mashed bananas (about 3 medium)
• 1 large egg
• ⅓ cup runny peanut butter 
• 2 tbsp maple syrup or honey
• 1 tsp vanilla extract
• 1 ¾ cups quick oats
• 1 tsp cinnamon
• ½ tsp salt
• ⅓ cup chocolate chips 

Directions

1️⃣ Preheat oven to 350°F and line a baking sheet with parchment paper.
2️⃣ Mash bananas in a bowl until smooth.
3️⃣ Stir in egg, peanut butter, maple syrup (or honey), and vanilla.
4️⃣ Add oats, cinnamon, and salt and mix until combined.
5️⃣ Fold in chocolate chips if using.
6️⃣ Scoop cookies onto the baking sheet and gently flatten.
7️⃣ Bake 12–15 minutes until set.

Let cool slightly and enjoy! These are soft, chewy, and naturally sweet from the bananas.

Hot Honey Beef Power Bowl 🍯🥑

Sweet + savory + creamy + HIGH protein. This one is on repeat and it’s perfect for meal prep—cook everything in bulk, portion it out, and you’ve got lunches ready for the week.

Recipe (1 bowl)
	•	112 g 7% lean ground beef (cooked + seasoned)
	•	90 g sweet potato, roasted (cubed)
	•	¼ avocado
	•	¼ cup 2% cottage cheese
	•	Hot honey drizzle (to taste)

How to make
	1.	Roast sweet potato cubes at 400°F for ~20–25 min (or air fry 375°F for 12–15 min) until tender.
	2.	Brown ground beef in a skillet, season with taco seasoning🔥).
	3.	Assemble: beef + sweet potatoes + cottage cheese + avocado.
	4.	Finish with a drizzle of hot honey.

Meal prep tip: keep cottage cheese, avocado + hot honey on the side and add right before eating for best texture.

💰 Résidents Corse, 

Avez vous entendu parler de l'aide financière  de l'AUE pour l'achat d'un Vélo électrique?

De quoi s'agit il?
C'est un coup de pouce financier pour les particuliers,  les collectivités ou même encore les entreprises pour rendre l'achat de VAE (Vélo à assistance électrique) plus accessible.

Quels sont les VAE concernés?
Seuls les vélos de ville ou les VTC sont concernés par ce dispositif.

Quel est le montant ?
Le montant de l'aide est attribué par l'AUE. Elle correspond à 25% du prix d'achat d'un VAE, plafonnée à 500€.

Pour plus d'informations, n'hésitez pas à consulter le lien en story à la une ou de vous rapprocher de nos conseillers cycles en magasin.

A très vite!

✨️ Voici 7 bonnes raisons de commencer la course à pieds 👟

Qui a dit que la course à pieds c'était facile? Qui peut dire qu'il a tout de suite aimer courir lors de sa 1ère sortie? 

La course à pied est une activité simple et accessible qui consiste à courir à son rythme pour améliorer sa condition physique et son bien-être. Au début, elle peut sembler difficile : le souffle est court, les jambes sont lourdes et la motivation peut parfois manquer. Les premières sorties demandent de la patience et de la persévérance pour habituer le corps à l’effort.

Mais au fil des entraînements, les progrès apparaissent : on court plus longtemps, plus facilement, et la fatigue laisse place à une vraie sensation de satisfaction. Terminer une sortie ou atteindre un nouvel objectif procure une grande fierté, donnant envie de continuer et de se dépasser. 🏃‍♂️✨

A fruit bowl might look pretty but it's ruining your produce! 😱

This combination of fruits will cause each other to go bad quicker. Bananas should always be stored alone, preferably on a banana hook on the counter. Apples and oranges will last longer in the fridge but keep them far apart. I prefer oranges in the crisper drawer and apples in a bowl on the top shelf. 🍌

Oranges and citrus can be stored with avocados, and apples can be stored near pears. But besides that, avoid keeping too many fruits in one place or you're just causing them to spoil. 🍊

Share this with someone who will benefit from this information! 🍎

Adding items to your grocery list as you are getting low, not completely out, allows you to have flexibility in when you are buying them. You can watch for a sale on the item to get it at the lowest price or order it in bulk without having to worry about shipping time. 

If you find yourself always needing to run to the store for items that you are out of, consider giving this method a try! It's just one of the ways that I'm able to stick to a grocery budget of $135/person per month.

This month when I went grocery shopping I accidentally went over budget by about $30. 

I was shopping for several events and holidays within this trip and decided to not make a separate line item for them. We also are halting guests in our home for a few days so we will be preparing larger meals. So for all of those extras, spending just $30 is actually not too bad. 

If you want to see my entire grocery haul and hear how I'm stretching out ingredients to be able to have parties and guests while only spending a little extra, comment VIDEO and I'll send you a link!

When I buy large packages of meat, I am intentional about spreading it out so that it can last for several months. I freeze it in portion sizes so that we are cooking just what we need. This allows us to still enjoy our favorite foods, even with rising grocery prices. Our grocery budget is $135/person per month and we have stuck to that for several years. 

Do you enjoy bacon? Have you noticed rising prices impacting your grocery spending? Share in the comments!

I bought these green onions a month ago and have been using them the entire month! Since it is spring, it is their growing season. Even as I use them, they continue to grow. Soon I will trim the ends so that I can store the cut onions in the freezer and have them to use for several months. All from buying just one bundle of green onions. 

Store them in a glass jar with filtered water on your conter or windowsill and watch them grow.

Anfang Februar vs. Ende Februar – minus 13 kg.

Und ehrlich gesagt: 
Mir war gar nicht bewusst, wie viel sich optisch verändert hat.

Wenn man sich jeden Tag im Spiegel sieht, merkt man viele Veränderungen einfach nicht. Alles fühlt sich irgendwie langsam und normal an.

Bis zu dem Moment, als mir etwas aufgefallen ist: 
Meine Hosen schlagen plötzlich Falten.

Sie sitzen lockerer, rutschen ein bisschen und auf einmal wird mir klar: 
Da hat sich wirklich etwas verändert.

Manchmal sieht man Fortschritte nicht sofort auf Fotos oder im Spiegel. 
Aber dann kommen diese kleinen Momente im Alltag, die einem zeigen:

Der Körper verändert sich. Schritt für Schritt.

Und genau diese Momente fühlen sich irgendwie besonders an. 🤍

Wer von euch kennt diesen besonderen Moment, 
wenn Kleidung plötzlich ganz anders sitzt?

Beginning of February vs. end of February – minus 13 kg.

And honestly… 
I didn’t even realize how much had changed visually.

When you see yourself in the mirror every day, it’s easy to miss the progress. Everything feels slow and normal.

Until one moment made me notice it:

My pants suddenly started forming folds.

They felt looser, started sliding a little, and that’s when it hit me: 
Something really has changed.

Sometimes progress doesn’t show up clearly in the mirror or in photos.

But then these small everyday moments happen and remind you:

Your body is changing. Step by step.

And those moments feel pretty special. 🤍

Have you ever had that moment 
when your clothes suddenly fit differently? 

#TrustTheProcess
#KleineSchritteGroßeVeränderung
#ProgressNotPerfection
#Abnehmreise
#LittleWins

High-protein rolls after bariatric surgery? Yes please! 🥯💪

If you’ve had a gastric bypass or another bariatric surgery, you know that even small things like bread can feel very different after the operation.

Before surgery, a bread roll was just a bread roll. 
Now I focus on foods that keep me full, provide enough protein, and feel good for my body.

These quark oat rolls are one of my favorite discoveries: 
🥯 quick to make 
💪 high in protein 
🤍 easy to tolerate after gastric bypass 

Recipes like this make everyday life after surgery so much easier — and they help me feel like I don’t have to miss out on the foods I love.

Nutrition (approx.)

Whole recipe: 
≈ 925 kcal 
≈ 62 g protein 

Per roll:

If you make 9 rolls 
≈ 103 kcal 
≈ 6.9 g protein 

If you make 8 rolls 
≈ 116 kcal 
≈ 7.8 g protein 

If you add 50 g feta: 
+ ≈ 130 kcal 
+ ≈ 7 g protein 

Whole recipe then: 
≈ 1055 kcal 
≈ 69 g protein

Like any other exercise, unlocking your pull-ups requires consistent work, but it builds more than just your upper body strength so you should not forget to include it in your routine. 

For those who want to work on theirs, you may start with variations or an assisted pull-up to build strength progressively. 

We are incredibly proud of everyone who joined our January pull-up challenge this year. It was never about doing every rep strictly, but diligently for every day.

This exercise may be one of the most challenging bodyweight movements and it is known that only a small percentage of the world's population can do a pull-up.

It's that time of the year again! 🥳

Let's welcome the year with the pull-up challenge to help you get moving.

We start with just one pull-up on day 1, building the habit  day by day and the strength eventually.

Whether you’re doing strict dead-hangs, assisted bands, or inverted rows, the goal is the same: Don’t break the chain.

Hello everyone 👋 for anyone who doesn’t know me my name is Tom. Im 36 and I’m passionate about helping other people on their fitness journey to become the best version of themselves. 

I always been into my health and fitness wanting to be the best version of me. I know the start to 2020 was a hard time for many:

❌ Struggling to keep accountable, 
❌ Gaining weight 
❌ Lack of drive 
❌ Giving up on themselves 
❌ Eating for the sake of it 

During the lockdown I did a fitness program online to help people get their drive and desire back; this training was all in the comfort of their own home, to prove you can have a good workout when when you don’t have time, money or access to gyms. On one of our sessions we did toilet roll stacks, using pots and pans for weights!

I’ve become more into my fitness and my motivation, seeing the positive in things instead of seeing the negative and not believing in myself.

My wife motivates me and inspires everyday to be the best husband I can be, I’m so grateful of her everyday. We got married 19 July 2024, we have a beautiful daughter who will be 2 in June. My girls keep me going everyday, to be the best dad and husband I can be. 

Since I became a daddy my fitness level has increased a lot and my love for my daughter and wife gets stronger and stronger every day. I see the positives in life.

I love to help people who have been struggling on their fitness journey and need motivation, but mainly parents who struggle to make time for themselves or focus on their fitness goals, getting takeaways, ready meals instead of fresh ingredients. If this is you I would love to help. Never give up on you. Be the best daddy/mummy, human being that you are. 

I’ve also recently started a podcast called ‘Tom’s real Pep Talks’ because I want to remind you that you’re better than you think…keep that amazing smile on your face. 

If this is something you have been struggling with and you are interested, DM me ‘MOTIVATION’, I would love to see if can help 💪

MOBILITY WORKOUT — PERFORMANCE FIRST 🔥
Mobility isn’t just about stretching.
It’s strength through range.
If your joints can’t control movement, your strength won’t translate —
and neither will your speed, power, or longevity.
⚡ THE FORMAT
8 Exercises
3 Sets
⏱ 1 Minute On
⏸ 30 Second Transition
🧠 WHY THIS MATTERS (THE DATA)
• Limited mobility can reduce force output by 10–30%
• End-range strength improves joint stability + injury resilience
• Controlled mobility work enhances efficiency + recovery
• Better mobility = improved sprint mechanics, squat depth, and shoulder health
🔥 REAL TRUTH
• Mobility is active control, not passive stretching
• Strength without mobility = higher injury risk
• More range + control = better performance under fatigue
You don’t lose strength getting mobile —
you unlock the strength you already have.
✅ HOW TO USE THIS
• Move slow
• Control every rep
• Own every position
• Breathe with intention

En regiones como Magallanes, donde el clima es más frío y el sedentarismo puede ser mayor, el entrenamiento no es solo una cuestión estética, sino una herramienta importante para cuidar la salud.

8 de cada 10 adultos tiene sobrepeso u obesidad

Significa que la mayoría de la población adulta presenta exceso de peso, lo que aumenta el riesgo de enfermedades metabólicas.

El sobrepeso y la obesidad no solo afectan la apariencia física. También aumentan significativamente el riesgo de enfermedades crónicas como:

-DIabetes tipo 2
-Hipertensión arterial
-Enfermedades cardiovasculares

Estas son algunas de las principales causas de mortalidad en Chile.

El problema empieza temprano ya que 66% de los niños tiene sobrepeso u obesidad.

Este problema no comienza en la adultez.
En Chile, estudios de nutrición escolar muestran que una gran proporción de niños ya presenta exceso de peso, lo que aumenta el riesgo de enfermedades en el futuro.

La diabetes tipo 2 es una enfermedad metabólica que está creciendo en todo el mundo.

En Chile, cerca del 12% de los adultos vive con diabetes, y muchos casos no están diagnosticados.

Uno de los factores más importantes detrás de estas enfermedades es la falta de actividad física.

El sedentarismo está asociado a:

-Obesidad
-Diabetes
-Enfermedades cardiovasculares
-Deterioro metabólico

El clima frío puede influir en el estilo de vida:

-Menos actividad física al aire libre
-Mayor tiempo en interiores
-Mayor consumo de alimentos calóricos

Esto puede favorecer el sedentarismo.

El entrenamiento regular ayuda a disminuir el porcentaje de grasa corporal, lo que mejora el metabolismo y reduce el riesgo de enfermedades.

El entrenamiento no es solo para verse bien físicamente, sino para prevenir enfermedades y mejorar la salud.

El ejercicio tiene beneficios que van más allá del físico, puede mejorar:

La salud mental
Los niveles de energía
La calidad de vida

Entrenar ayuda a:
❤️ Vivir más sano
🧠 Tener más energía
📈 Mejorar la calidad de vida

💪 ¿Las mujeres se vuelven muy musculosas por levantar pesas?

Este es uno de los mitos más grandes del fitness.

La ciencia muestra algo muy claro:

🧬 Las mujeres tienen aproximadamente
10–20 veces menos testosterona que los hombres.

¿Que significa esto?

✔ pueden ganar fuerza
✔ pueden ganar músculo
✔ pueden mejorar su composición corporal

Pero ❗

❌ no desarrollan músculos enormes fácilmente

Esto no es una opinión.
Es lo que muestran meta-análisis y estudios científicos sobre entrenamiento de fuerza.

De hecho, la evidencia muestra que:

👉 las mujeres ganan músculo en proporción similar a los hombres cuando entrenan.

La diferencia principal es hormonal, no de capacidad.

Por eso el entrenamiento de fuerza en mujeres ayuda a:

💪 mejorar fuerza
🔥 reducir grasa corporal
🦴 mejorar salud ósea
⚡ aumentar metabolismo

Entrenar pesas no te hace “demasiado musculosa”.

🥩 ¿Cuánta proteína necesitas realmente para ganar músculo?

Durante años se repitió la recomendación mínima de 0.8 g/kg, pero la ciencia actual muestra algo muy diferente.

📚 Meta-análisis científicos sobre entrenamiento y nutrición han encontrado que:

👉 1.6 g/kg/día maximiza el crecimiento muscular
y que para personas activas el rango ideal suele estar entre: 1.6 – 2.2 g/kg/día

Esto significa que una persona de 70 kg podría necesitar aproximadamente: 112 – 154 g de proteína al día.

Pero hay algo importante que muchos no saben:

📊 Consumir más proteína no siempre significa más músculo.

Existe un punto de saturación, donde los beneficios empiezan a estabilizarse. Por eso los estudios también recomiendan distribuir la proteína durante el día:

👉 0.24 – 0.40 g/kg por comida
para optimizar la síntesis de proteína muscular.

💡 Conclusión científica:
✔ la proteína es esencial para ganar músculo
✔ el entrenamiento de fuerza es el estímulo principal
✔ el rango ideal para personas activas suele ser:
🔥 1.6 – 2.2 g/kg/día

🔥 HIIT vs Cardio tradicional

Durante años se ha discutido qué entrenamiento quema más grasa.

¿HIIT o cardio moderado?

Para responder esto, investigadores analizaron 29 estudios científicos con 807 personas comparando:

🔥 HIIT (High Intensity Interval Training)
🏃 Cardio moderado continuo

📊 ¿Qué encontraron?

Ambos tipos de entrenamiento:

✔ reducen grasa corporal
✔ ayudan a bajar de peso
✔ mejoran la salud cardiovascular

Pero encontraron algo interesante…

👉 El HIIT puede lograr resultados similares en menos tiempo.

🧬 ¿Por qué ocurre esto?

El HIIT genera adaptaciones metabólicas más fuertes porque:

✔ activa más fibras musculares
✔ aumenta el gasto energético
✔ eleva el metabolismo

Además, después de un entrenamiento HIIT ocurre algo llamado EPOC.

Esto significa que tu metabolismo sigue elevado incluso horas después del ejercicio, aumentando el gasto energético total.

🔬 Otro dato importante del estudio

El HIIT puede ser especialmente útil para reducir grasa abdominal, particularmente en personas con sobrepeso u obesidad.

También puede ayudar a:

✔ mejorar la sensibilidad a la insulina
✔ mejorar el uso de glucosa
✔ reducir la acumulación de grasa

⚠️ Pero la ciencia deja algo claro

❌ No existe un cardio mágico.

La clave siempre será:

✔ constancia
✔ buena alimentación
✔ entrenamiento regular

🔥 Conclusión

HIIT → más eficiente en tiempo
Cardio moderado → más fácil de sostener

👉 Ambos funcionan.

💬 Ahora te pregunto

¿Tú qué prefieres entrenar?

Perimenopause - the years leading up to that 1 year where hormones fluctuate, which for some is going to be quick, for others it can be YEARS

You have 3 types of cells on the follicles on your ovaries.

One is responsible for the release of testosterone and other androgens.

One is responsible for the release of estrogen.

And one is responsible for the release of progesterone.

We are born with all of our follicles and eventually we will run out.

Which means we will slowly stop producing those hormones.

But it isn’t just an on/off switch.

It is going to fluctuate - some days you can have lots of estrogen and then other days have none.

This is why some days you feel good and other days you want to set the world on fire

We end up all these dips and valleys kind of like a roller coaster!

What we want to do is minimize the highs and lows so your symptoms are less extreme and so you can lose weight!

The key here is CORTISOL

Because when cortisol is high (our stress hormone) it is going to negatively influence our estrogen levels, decrease progesterone, and bunch of other things like increasing insulin resistance, decrease thyroid function, increasing inflammation, reducing digestion = bloating and ultimately all of this is going to lead to belly fat accumulate AND more hormonal symptoms.

PLUS when estrogen decreases our stress tolerance is already reduced so we are already dealing with the above issues of increased cortisol so adding more fuel to fire is going to make this rollercoaster more crazy = more severe symptoms and weight loss resistance.

So what can you do to lower cortisol?
1. Eat enough carbs
2. Eat enough food 
3. Prioritize sleep
4. Limit alcohol
5. Limit caffeine intake
6. Adjust your training (cardio and HIIT and high intensity exercise will increase your cortisol and decrease muscle mass, try opting for strength training with longer rest periods)
7. No fasting 
8. De-stress midday (literally lay down for 5 minutes and focus on deep slow breathing)

One hormone that becomes unstable in perimenopause may be driving it.

👉 It’s called aldosterone.

👉 And it regulates sodium, potassium, fluid balance, and blood pressure.

When aldosterone swings, women often experience:

✔️bloating
✔️puffy face in the morning
✔️swollen fingers or ankles
✔️breast tenderness
✔️headaches or pressure behind the eyes
✔️weight that fluctuates overnight

Most women are told to:

❌ drink more water
❌ cut salt
❌ cut carbs

But hydration is not just about water intake.

It’s about electrolytes and hormonal signaling.

Here are a few ways to stabilize fluid balance during perimenopause:

1️⃣ Stop fasting all morning

Low-carb or long fasting windows can worsen electrolyte instability and cortisol spikes, which increases fluid swings.

Aim for a protein + carb meal within a 90 minutes of waking BEFORE coffee

2️⃣ Reduce caffeine load

Caffeine temporarily increases aldosterone activity.
Multiple coffees per day can make fluid retention worse.

3️⃣ Support progesterone

Progesterone helps stabilize aldosterone.

Focus on:
✔️consistent meals (3 meals a day 4-5 hours apart)
✔️adequate calories
✔️magnesium (leafy greens, avocado, potatoes, pumpkin seeds, salmon)
✔️B6-rich foods (salmon, potatoes, bananas, avocado, chicken breast, pistachios)

Here’s the piece most women don’t hear about.

Progesterone helps stabilize aldosterone.

When progesterone is healthy, it naturally balances fluid regulation, helping your body manage sodium and water properly.

Aldosterone becomes more erratic.

Your bloating.
Your 3am wake-ups.
Your heavy periods.
Your stubborn lower-belly fat.

It might not be “just perimenopause.”

It might be your liver struggling to keep up.

In perimenopause, estrogen fluctuates higher and more unpredictably.
If your liver can’t metabolize and clear it efficiently → it recirculates.

And when that happens, symptoms get louder:

- Water retention
- Breast tenderness
- Mood swings
- Constipation
- Histamine flares
- Night waking
- Fat loss resistance

This isn’t about detox teas.
It’s about supporting Phase 1 and Phase 2 estrogen clearance so your body can actually regulate hormones properly.

Here’s where to start 👇

1️⃣ Increase protein to ~0.8g per lb bodyweight

You need amino acids (glycine, taurine, cysteine) for Phase 2 detox pathways👇🏽

- Chicken breast
- Turkey
- White fish (cod, halibut)
- Egg whites
- Non-fat plain Greek yogurt
- Lean ground beef

2️⃣ Hit 25–35g fiber daily

Fiber binds metabolized estrogen in the gut so you don’t reabsorb it.
Constipation = estrogen recycling.

📗Grab our fiber guide for a full list - comment “FIBER”

3️⃣ Stabilize blood sugar

High insulin slows fat burning and stresses liver detox capacity.

💡Tip: Eat some protein + veggies first before digging into carbs.

💡Tip: Eat every 4-5 hours apart and make sure to eat breakfast

4️⃣ Support bile flow

👟Daily movement
🍋Hydration (Lemon water, dandelion tea)
🥬Bitter greens (arugula, radicchio)
🥦Cooked cruciferous vegetables.

5️⃣ Eat Enough Calories

When calories stay too low:

- Estrogen clearance slows
- T4 → T3 conversion drops
- Cortisol rises to keep blood sugar stable
- Fat burning downshifts and you start gaining weight

👉 Eating less isn’t always the solution. It’s the stressor.

Perimenopause weight gain, bloating, insomnia, estrogen dominance symptoms, cortisol belly 👉 they are all connected through physiology.

📑High‑intensity interval training (HIIT) and moderate‑to‑vigorous‑intensity continuous training (MVICT) are widely used to improve aerobic fitness and physical performance. However, evidence inconclusively comparing their long‑term physiological adaptations across different populations and training protocols remains inconsistent.

📊Our New Meta-analysis of 115 randomized controlled trials involving 3,196 participants (ages 8–68; from inactive individuals to elite athletes).

🫀HIIT showed greater improvements than moderate‑to‑vigorous continuous training (MVICT) in VO₂max, maximal aerobic power/speed, and mean anaerobic power.

🏃No significant differences were observed between HIIT and MVICT for intensity thresholds, exercise economy, or overall physical performance.

🚣Moderator analyses indicated that outcomes were influenced by training status, age, sex, interval type, and exercise mode.

🎯Training adaptations vary across individuals, and exercise prescription should be individualized rather than “one size fits all.”

🍫 ZealFitt Cosmic Chocolate Protein Ice Cream Waffle Tacos

Low-Cal Sweet Treat | High-Protein Dessert Hack

Viral waffle tacos… but make it ZealFitt macro-friendly 👀🔥
These Cosmic-style chocolate ice cream tacos are only 137 calories each and perfect for low-calorie meal prep when you’re cutting but still want something sweet.

⸻

📊 ZealFitt Macros (Per Taco + Toppings)

137 Calories
19g Carbs | 5g Fat | 4g Protein

📊 ZealFitt Macros (Without Chocolate Dip & Toppings)

73 Calories
10g Carbs | 2g Fat | 4g Protein

⸻

🛒 Ingredients (Makes 15 ZealFitt Ice Cream Tacos)

For the Protein Ice Cream Filling:
	•	45g Chocolate Protein Powder (whey/casein blend works best)
	•	15g Dark Cocoa Powder
	•	8g Sugar-Free Chocolate Pudding Mix
	•	5g Zero-Calorie Sweetener
	•	~215g Fat-Free Whipped Topping
	•	Cold water (as needed)

For the Taco Shells & Toppings:
	•	15 Thin Dark Chocolate Pizzelle Cookies
	•	120g Dark Chocolate Chips (for dipping)
	•	75g Chocolate Brownie Bits or Chocolate Sprinkles

⸻

👨🏻‍🍳 ZealFitt Method

1️⃣ Shape the Waffle Taco Shells
	•	Place one pizzelle between paper towels.
	•	Microwave for 20–25 seconds until soft.
	•	Immediately fold into a taco shape (they harden FAST).
	•	Repeat for all 15.

2️⃣ Make the Protein Ice Cream Mix
	•	Combine protein powder, cocoa, pudding mix, and sweetener in a bowl.
	•	Slowly mix in cold water until smooth and slightly runny (like thick icing).
	•	Gently fold in the fat-free whipped topping.
	•	Don’t overmix — keep it fluffy.

3️⃣ Fill & Freeze
	•	Evenly spoon mixture into taco shells.
	•	Freeze for 3–4 hours until solid.

4️⃣ Chocolate Dip
	•	Melt dark chocolate chips.
	•	Dip tops of frozen tacos.
	•	Add brownie bits or sprinkles.

5️⃣ Final Freeze
	•	Freeze another 30–60 minutes.
	•	Store in freezer and grab whenever sweet cravings hit.

⸻

🔥 ZealFitt Tip

This version is perfect for meal prep because it freezes well and stays scoopable once set.

You can also:
	•	Use low-calorie ice cream instead of the protein blend
	•	Add peanut butter drizzle
	•	Use white chocolate for a different macro split

ZealFitt High-Protein Breakfast Bagels 🥯🔥

Bacon, Egg & Cheese Meal Prep – 41g Protein Per Serve 💪

The ultimate grab-and-go high-protein breakfast. Soft, cheesy egg layers stacked with crispy bacon inside toasted bagels — perfect for busy mornings while staying on track with your macros.

⸻

Macros (Per Serving – 8 Serves)

485 Calories
35g Carbs | 16.5g Fat | 41g Protein

⸻

Ingredients

Egg Layer

• 12 whole eggs
• 500g egg whites
• 200g blended low-fat cottage cheese
• 5g grated parmesan
• 1 tsp garlic powder
• 1 tsp salt

⸻

Fillings

• 140g low-fat cheddar cheese
• 16 slices bacon (cut in half once cooked – use lean bacon, turkey, or beef bacon)
• 150g light or fat-free cream cheese
• ½ tsp dried chives

⸻

Base

• 8 bagels (low-carb or bagel thins work great)

⸻

Instructions

1. Cook the Egg Mixture

In a large bowl whisk together eggs, egg whites, blended cottage cheese, parmesan, garlic powder, and salt.

Pour into a lined baking tray and bake at 180°C / 350°F until set.

Allow to cool slightly, then slice into 8 even portions.

⸻

2. Cook the Bacon

Cook bacon until crispy using a pan, oven, or air fryer.

Set aside and cut each slice in half.

⸻

3. Build the Bagels

Slice bagels in half.

Spread cream cheese on each side, then layer:
Egg → cheese → bacon → sprinkle of chives.

Close the bagels.

⸻

4. Meal Prep

Wrap each bagel in baking paper and store in the fridge for the week.

⸻

ZealFitt High-Protein Sour Cream & Onion Crunch Chips 🥔🔥

Low Calorie Fakeaway Snack

Crispy, flavour-packed and ridiculously easy to make. These ZealFitt homemade chips give you that classic sour cream & onion flavour while keeping the calories low and the macros macro-friendly.

Perfect for a healthy snack, meal prep side, or when you’re craving chips without blowing your calories.

Macros (Entire Batch)
293 Calories
57g Carbs | 4g Fat | 14g Protein

⸻

Ingredients

• 500g peeled potatoes, sliced very thin (mandolin recommended for even slices)
• Bowl of ice water (helps remove excess starch for extra crispiness)
• Light cooking oil spray

⸻

ZealFitt Sour Cream & Onion Seasoning

• ½ tbsp salt
• 1 tsp dried parsley
• 1 tsp garlic powder
• 2 tsp onion powder
• 1 tsp natural sweetener (stevia, monk fruit or similar)
• ½ tbsp buttermilk powder
• ½ tsp dried dill

⸻

Instructions
	1.	Slice the potatoes as thin as possible using a mandolin or sharp knife.
	2.	Place slices into a bowl of cold water and ice for 10–15 minutes to remove excess starch.
	3.	Drain and pat completely dry with paper towel.
	4.	Lay the potato slices evenly in the air fryer or on a baking tray.
	5.	Lightly spray with cooking oil.
	6.	Cook using one of the methods below.
	7.	Once crispy, sprinkle with the seasoning mix and toss until evenly coated.

Cooking Guide

Air Fryer:
200°C / 400°F for 10–15 minutes

Oven:
200°C / 400°F for 15–25 minutes

ZealFitt Tip 💡
