ZealFitt Korean-Style Crispy Chicken Mac & Cheese 🍗🧀🔥

High-Protein Comfort Meal Prep – 65g Protein Per Serve 💪

Crispy Korean-inspired chicken tossed in a sweet, spicy glaze and served with a creamy high-protein mac & cheese. This ZealFitt meal prep delivers serious flavour while keeping your macros on point.
Macros (Per Serving – 4 Serves)

680 Calories
82g Carbs | 11g Fat | 65g Protein

⸻

Ingredients

Crispy Chicken

• 700g boneless skinless chicken breast, diced
• 1 egg
• 10g minced garlic
• 10g minced ginger
• ½ tsp white pepper
• 1 tsp salt
• 150g potato starch (only around 40g sticks to the chicken)
• Light cooking oil spray

⸻

Korean-Style Glaze

• 90ml reduced salt soy sauce
• 90ml water
• 20g brown natural sweetener
• 10g minced garlic
• 10g minced ginger
• 2–3 tsp gochujang chilli paste
• 1 tsp potato starch or cornflour mixed with water
• 1 tsp chilli flakes

⸻

High-Protein Mac & Cheese

• 340ml fat-free evaporated milk
• 1 tsp smoked paprika
• 1 tsp low sodium chicken stock powder
• 80g light cream cheese
• 100g light grated cheddar cheese
• 320g macaroni pasta (dry weight)

⸻

Instructions

1. Prepare the Chicken

In a bowl combine chicken, egg, garlic, ginger, white pepper, and salt.
Mix well so the chicken is evenly coated.

Add potato starch and toss until the chicken pieces are lightly coated.

Air fry or oven cook at 200°C / 400°F until golden and crispy.

⸻

2. Make the Korean Glaze

Add soy sauce, water, sweetener, garlic, ginger, gochujang, and chilli flakes to a pan.

Simmer over medium heat.

Stir in the cornflour mixture and cook until the sauce thickens.

Toss the crispy chicken in the glaze until coated.

⸻

3. Cook the Pasta

Boil macaroni in salted water until tender.

Drain and set aside.

⸻

4. Make the Cheese Sauce

In a saucepan add evaporated milk, smoked paprika, and chicken stock powder.

Heat gently and stir in cream cheese until smooth.

Add grated cheddar and stir until melted and creamy.

Mix the cooked pasta through the sauce.

⸻

5. Portion for Meal Prep

Divide mac & cheese into 4 containers and top with Korean chicken.

Store in the fridge for easy high-protein meals during the week.

ZealFitt Crispy Honey Garlic Butter Chicken & Rice 🍗🍯🍚

High-Protein Meal Prep | 55g Protein Per Serve 💪

Sweet, savoury and crispy — this ZealFitt honey garlic chicken is the perfect macro-friendly meal prep. Packed with lean protein and flavour while staying low in fat so you can stay on track with your goals.

Macros (Per Serving – 6 Serves)
563 Calories
72g Carbs | 5.6g Fat | 55g Protein
⸻
Ingredients

Chicken
	•	1.2kg boneless skinless chicken breast, diced (raw weight)
	•	1 tsp garlic powder
	•	½ tsp black pepper
	•	35ml reduced-salt soy sauce
	•	1 egg, whisked
	•	150g potato starch or cornflour (only about 50g will stick to the chicken)
	•	Light cooking oil spray

Rice
	•	350g white rice (uncooked weight)
	•	750ml chicken broth

Honey Garlic Butter Sauce
	•	100g honey
	•	60ml reduced-salt soy sauce
	•	60ml water
	•	6 garlic cloves, finely diced
	•	1 tsp potato starch or cornflour
	•	40g light butter
⸻
Instructions
	1.	Cook the rice:
Add the rice and chicken broth to a rice cooker or pot and cook until fluffy.
	2.	Season the chicken:
In a bowl combine the diced chicken with garlic powder, black pepper and soy sauce. Mix well.
	3.	Coat the chicken:
Add the whisked egg and mix until coated. Toss the chicken through the potato starch so each piece is lightly coated.
	4.	Cook the chicken:
Place chicken pieces in an air fryer or large pan, spray lightly with cooking spray and cook until golden and crispy.
	5.	Make the sauce:
In a pan combine honey, soy sauce, water and diced garlic. Bring to a gentle simmer.
	6.	Thicken:
Mix the cornflour with a little water and add to the sauce. Stir until it thickens.
	7.	Finish the sauce:
Add the light butter and stir until melted and glossy.
	8.	Combine:
Toss the crispy chicken through the honey garlic butter sauce until evenly coated.
	9.	Meal prep:
Divide rice into 6 containers, top with the honey garlic chicken and store in the fridge.
⸻
ZealFitt Tip

Reheat in the microwave and add a small splash of water to the rice to keep it fluffy.
